I then joined the Army after college. I originally joined as an officer and then transitioned to a NCO. I worked in security while I was in the Army. My duties included time at the guard post and base security at a missile site.

In college I studied forensic psychology. In addition I studied intelligence analyst, criminology, sociology, and psychology. I took many courses in law enforcement. I am also a legal assistant.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: No matter who you are, at the end of the day you are left with yourself. I look at it as the “big picture” and all of its implications. So, to be clear I am 29 years old, I was born and raised in Virginia by a single mother. I grew up in a nice neighborhood and attended one of the best schools in the state. At that time my father was dead to me, and the man who had walked out on my mother had never been of value to me. I was part of a small and very close knit family as my mother remarried when I was four years old. My siblings are my closest friends as well. I am who I am for a lot of reasons, and being 100% brutally honest I make no apologies for any of it. Like all people, my past was just that and I was born with the hand I was dealt. That being said I made the most of it, I wanted more. I made a deliberate choice to leave my comfort zone to experience the world in order to become more. I enjoy what people call leadership, it’s my “calling.” I have worked for many years at a large management consulting firm, and now I am at the government. I understand people and I have developed a keen sense of awareness. I have changed who I am to be useful to those around me and have been happy in doing so. I am an extremely altruistic person, I have no idea why I’m like that. This world is just… wrong on a bunch of levels. I would do anything for anyone in need without question. Everyone I know has some dysfunctional piece of their life, yet, they all have one common thread; they can call on me, and I’ll always be there.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
Hello, my name is Joseph Maximus Thayer. I am 23 years old. I was born to Robert Paul Thayer and Vanessa Ana Fernandez in Madison, Wisconsin in July of 1995. Growing up in rural northwestern Wisconsin has given me an appreciation for all things outdoors. Nothing beats a quiet summer evening playing guitar and drinking beer around a bonfire, although I have learned not to burn down the family cabin, as I did when I was about 10 years old. Since my father has two other children, I consider myself the middle child. An advantage of that is that I can escape the house when I want. My parents have always supported me and the decisions I have made. When I was 19 and began not to get along with my father, they let me move to Nebraska to help my uncle build a house. I have always been independent, and they trust in me to do the right thing.

Now I’m about to become one of the youngest graduate students ever to graduate from the University of Nebraska-Lincoln with a degree in Meteorology. I have always had an interest in science and nature, but it really took off when I got home after a family trip to Yellowstone and Grand Teton National Park. When my family looked at the map and saw all of the geysers and volcanic remnants in the area, we decided that we had to hike through the Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone, and that is where I saw Old Faithful for the first time. I really wanted to be up close to that activity, and so my interest in science was piqued. When we got home, I began watching the weather channel. I learned what all of the instruments were on the weather map and what everything meant. I was so fascinated that I began exploring weather further. I was amazed at how easy it was to download satellite weather imagery from the World Meteorological Organization, and I had fun pretending that I was a real meteorologist, and that I was having to make predictions about what weather I was supposed to have. That was a pretty fun experiment, and I learned that no matter what, there’s always something interesting going on up in the atmosphere.

Eventually, I was advanced enough in my investigation of weather and how it works that I wanted to take meteorology courses at the University of Wisconsin-Stout. At first, my family did not understand that, but after seeing how hard I was working in my Internet courses, and doing other things with weather like raising weather balloons, they began to understand. I am probably the only person that I know that actually enjoys doing their homework and researching things on the Internet for class assignments.

I quickly got into the courses and began doing well. I loved it. My instructors and classmates were very supportive of me and were very encouraging. I was soon elected to be President of the Meteorology Club. It was from my very first course, Introduction to Meteorology, when I was the only student in the class, that my course grade went up in an upward slope, as they would call it in the weather. It was there that I knew that I would always be a meteorologist. With that same upward slope, I got a job at Camp Ripley as a WxBanker, a job that lasted about 2 months, just long enough for me to see the effects of my efforts. I was always very interactive with the participants in the training seminars, the radars, the satellites, and the weather balloons, when I thought about what the various instruments meant. Some people even came to me for advice when they were doing their assessments at the end of the class.

By the time I had completed my meteorology classes, I realized that I wanted to get into the National Weather Service, and so I set that goal. A particular instructor at Stout told me that the NWS is looking for graduate students because they know that graduate school teaches you more than undergrad does and that their internships work for school credit, and so he encouraged me to take a look. After learning more about it, I found out that my undergraduate degree will be in a very valuable degree for the NWS. We have lots of weather events, such as severe storms, tornadoes, flash floods, and I’ve also heard of hailstorms. But you have to wonder why there aren’t more major weather events. Certainly it is possible. It just seems that more planning and communication is needed on the NWS’s part.

Graduation came, and my grandfather drove from Minnesota to get me. We got a hotel for the night, and then the next day had lunch, and then took me to Stout. I’ll always remember the looks on his and my parents’ faces. I have often taken my parents for granted. I realize now that I have to learn to be more independent, so that I am not burdened with my parents when I get married and have children.

In my internships, I learned not to always take everything at face value. I quickly learned that there are constant debates among the meteorologists about what course, speed, strength, and location will be of certain weather events. I learned that there are two schools of thought about the whole issue. Then I heard a strange thing. I was in a class on how to forecast T-storms, when we learned about all of the instruments used in forecasting. I was surprised to hear about something called Tropical Weather Analysis and Forecasting, and that information from this method could be used for T-storm forecasting, but that it is ignored by many meteorologists. One gentleman explained to me that it is used by real meteorologists. In fact, that is the way I have begun to formulate my thoughts about forecasting.

It is about time that I begin writing my thesis on extreme weather events, as I have called them. I hope to find that there are not that many of them, but that there are more than most people think there are.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 27 years old and was born on May 7, 1974, at 2:40 PM.

From my earliest recollection I can remember, in no particular order,

I am not close to any of my grandparents.

Growing up, I didn't want to do anything but play baseball.  Around age 10 or 11, I started following the St. Louis Cardinals because of their talent and then as they grew old, it was their heart.  This won't be the case anymore because I now follow the Atlanta Braves, the team I played for in little league.

From 1975 to the present day, the most important person in my life is Jane,  18 months old,  the daughter of my best friend.

About 15 years ago I had a heart condition that went undiagnosed for years.  In December of 1989, I had emergency surgery to repair a congenital defect in my aorta.  Because of my failure to seek medical attention, it went undiagnosed for 10 years.

At age 15 or 16 I began doing things that I would not like to admit to doing now.  They included associating with persons that I shouldn't, drinking, smoking marijuana, smoking cigarettes, taking drugs, and spending far too much time with a girl in the neighborhood that I had to much interest in.

I became friends with Wayne on the day that I moved to our neighborhood.  My parents and his parents lived at the end of the street and Wayne lived at the other end.  We spent hours on hours playing stick ball and hanging out behind the drug store.  Wayne has always been the one person in my life that I am closest to and always have been.  It is he that I call when I'm in need of someone to talk to and it is he that I trust most.

Something else that plays a big role in my life is working out.  I try to work out several times a week.  I became very interested in fitness after I saw the movie Rocky III.  I don't look quite like Stallone but I do keep myself in reasonably good shape.  I enjoy working out and I never walk into a gym without a warm up.  I find it better to work on my upper body and calves first and then follow that with some light stretching and a warm down.  Many people think it's hard to maintain a workout program with a busy schedule but it's really not.  I find that if I don't get at least 20 minutes of exercise in my day has not been productive.

My mornings start with a bowl of wheat germ, six or seven large strawberries, and a coffee enema.  Yes, you read correctly.  Coffee enema.  You've got to try one of these.  They are quite possibly the best way to start the day.  Once I have completed this and gotten the hot water heater going, it is time for the tele.

I have seen every episode of the original "Twilight Zone".  I don't watch the new series because it doesn't touch the classic.  Anyone who doesn't like this series must have problems of their own.

While I am a very serious person, sometimes I just can't help cracking a smile or laughing.  I can laugh at almost anything but do not tell any jokes.  I find that jokes do not work well when they are told.  I also can't tell when people are joking with me.  An example of this would be the time that I seriously thought that Mike Forman's whole house was exploding because someone had placed a "curtains bomb" in his house.  This bomb is an explosive similar to "cigarette bombs" except that it can only be triggered by curtains.  To make matters worse, Mike wouldn't tell me what happened because he found it so funny.

My father was an alcoholic but he is no longer.  It has taken a long time but he is doing much better now.

I believe that anyone is capable of doing anything they set their minds to and if you work at it long enough and hard enough, you will succeed.

Everyone gets obstacles in their life and they affect them differently.  Sometimes, the only way to get over them is to make the decision to do so.  The key is your mindset.  If you're down, you're done.

Myself included, have a habit of expecting to much from other people but not from ourselves.  When we don't meet our own expectations, we become frustrated.  This leads to a "I don't give a fuck" attitude and, from there, we fall into the downward spiral to trouble.

Work, school, friends, and exercise are very important to me.  I don't always have time for school, which upsets me.  I try very hard to keep in touch with my friends but I find it hard to believe that I can only remember to call them when I am up for a monthly limit in which I pay by the second.

I am currently in possession of a flawless digital watch.  On April 10, 1999, it stopped working.  It kept perfect time until this one day when the seconds simply stopped moving.  I didn't want to throw it away so, with the help of 30 tries and some rubbing alcohol, it now is my most expensive calculator watch.

One of my greatest possessions is my Denali expedition crampons.  These are a pair of steel pincers that hook onto my boots and let me walk on ice that is 4000 feet above sea level.  On a good day, I can walk 1000 feet vertically with these things.

The strangest part of my personality is that I am a man who cares about nothing and nobody but myself.  I don't care about other people.  To the best of my knowledge, I haven't felt guilt about something I've done for a long time.

I am always very happy and content and never feel blue.  I know that someday, something bad will happen to me.  I live my life making sure that I don't owe anyone anything and I always look at life from every angle.

I think I know who I am but maybe I don't.  All I know is that I'm happy and that I live my life being responsible for my own actions and that I treat everyone equally, regardless of their position.  In my opinion, this doesn't always work.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I’m 21 years old and I was born in Hong Kong. My father is a Chinese and my mother is a Malaysian-Chinese. I lived in Hong Kong from my birth to 9 years old. I moved to Malaysia at 9 and lived there until I turned 17. At the age of 17, I moved to Melbourne for my VCE. Now, I’m a final year Bachelor of Communications student majoring in Public Relations. I’m doing my internship in Edelman Malaysia and this would be my last semester in university. I am half Cantonese and half Hokkien. I came from a humble background where my father earned his living as a fruit seller and my mother took care of the house and me. I am the only daughter in the family with two elder brothers. I value family more than anything else in the world. I like travelling and reading books.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 27 years old, I grew up in a town called Cape Coral, which is 3rd largest in Florida. I grew up swimming in rivers, lakes and the Gulf of Mexico.

Fun times with the neighborhood kids. Different friends came and went since I grew up as a child of a U.S. Navy “Nuke” or nucular, some call it… lol. My folks were in the Navy and the issues for military families are easier seen than talked about since the constant upheaval caused by their daily movement among communities is the constant.

I attended a total of 7 schools growing up and as such made about 17 moves in my childhood. I never had a significant amount of childhood friends that carried through to my teenage years.

I began my efforts at becoming a musician in grade 5 with my keyboard, and at age 9 my first guitar. Singing came naturally and as one of my best friends since 2nd grade is also a musician. We wrote songs and performed in talent shows during middle and high school, entering high school’s talent show together singing my original song titled “A Thousand Faces”.

At 16 I was asked by a computer wiz that worked for a local radio station to teach myself how to write and deliver news and deliver it on the stations internet radio feed with regards to the uprising of newly signed class fighters for K1 tournaments. There were 5 shows done in all and I spent a great deal of time between classes and after school with the music major/wizard, he even schooled me on music, and pushed me to perform live and in audio project settings, he unfortunately passed away shortly after. Today I tell most of my creative, and news and opinion based writing, exclusively to the readers of CyprianStar6.com .com, with lots of support, tips and criticism from my “internet brothers” as I see them.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I grew up in Silicon Valley, CA. I had 5 brothers and 1 sister. There were 3 girls and 5 boys. All my life I was an academic "goody goody" without much drive to go out and experience life. When I was 18 years old, I saw an alien abduction video on TV. I'm not talking about reports of sighting aliens--these were videos by the aliens themselves on the abduction experience. I was fascinated by them. The video showed 3 aliens in a ship using machinery to extract eggs from human bodies. This clicked in me instantly and I told my mother about it. She thought it was ridiculous--told me not to think those things. So I shut it up right away. A few years later in 1999, there was a documentary on Art Bell's radio show hosted by William Lyne. The guest was a defector from Area 51. In the show, he described a "ultra secret base" where he once visited in the Mojave Desert. He said he saw aliens sitting on a long table in the middle of the room being examined by what looked like microwave emitters on a robotic cart. That was it. The show got shut down and I didn't know what to think. I got older and tired of my job in San Francisco. I was working in insurance but hated it. I sold everything I owned and moved to Thailand. Ever since I've been into esoterica and Ancient Aliens. I can't explain it but there is something inside of me that is connected to whatever this phenomenon is. I wouldn't be surprised if I was abducted some day.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: Well, well, here I am. I am 25 years old and a decade older then the previous version of myself. I grew up in France and the US since my parents split when I was four. At 13, I went to private boarding school in England and have spent the last 7 years trying to get back to France. I want to be a filmmaker. I am stuck in LA working for an editor. I have never shot a short film. I have never studied any aspect of film production. I have never developed my own work. I am bored and lonely and live in LA because I am too lazy to move. I am just trying to figure things out and I don’t know where to start. Yet. Oh wait. I lie. There is a project that I have been writing for the last 4 years. It’s my only piece of filmmaking. It’s pretty bad. I haven’t touched it in a year. If I survive graduate school, maybe I will come back to it.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m 21 years old, a female. I was born in Massachusetts. My father, who is no longer around, was in the US Coast Guard. My mother stayed in Massachusetts. I lived with her and my stepfather in the city of Boston. I went to Catholic school up until the age of 15, until I left home, and moved to the town of North Reading. I got my GED at North Reading High School. I dated the same guy for five years, who I met in Catholic school. I don’t like talking about my dad because I never knew him well, and was very young. But, I will say that he is an inspiration to me, from what little I did get to know about him, and what I know about him as a Marine.

My mother’s father was Jewish, and that has had an impact on my life since birth. I have Jewish blood, and there is no denying this fact. I embrace it, and I love being able to incorporate Jewish customs into my own spirituality, and growing up as an Orthodox Christian.

I used to have the misfortune of being homosexual, but luckily I saw the light of God and became straight. My worldview used to be liberal, but my love for Jesus has changed me completely.

As for what is important to me, I would have to say that the love of Christ, the love of my family, the love of my friends, my land, and my future are what matters most.

As for what I enjoy, I enjoy playing the drums, cooking, baking, writing, spending time with my family and friends. I have a very stressful job, and I am very introverted and I enjoy my privacy.

I’d also like to point out, the definition of homophobia. It’s not just hate towards homosexuals, it’s a lack of acceptance of homosexuals and their choices in life. Since I have been newly born again, I have learned about the homosexual lifestyle. It is not acceptable to God, and we are not to accept such choices in life. Sorry, it’s just the way it is. It’s very similar to drunk driving. People in this day and age don’t understand how it’s wrong, because they are so brainwashed. But, the fact is, that’s how we used to see it 60 years ago. People used to get very harsh sentences and even put in prison. Things are changing now because of political correctness. If we keep feeding it to the public, we will make it seem acceptable to God when it’s really not.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am a 20 years old Korean American from Daly City, California. I was born in Busan, South Korea but moved to CA when I was 4. When I was in 6th grade, I was diagnosed with PCOS (polycystic ovary syndrome). I was never really a healthy individual, but PCOS is a genetic disease that made me that much more sick. Ever since I started puberty in 7th grade, my body has reacted very negatively to the hormonal changes and it made me feel suicidal several times in the last 5 years. With medicine, therapy, and school work, I made it through high school with a 4.0 GPA and a full paid scholarship to Santa Clara University. At SCU, I am an undergraduate biochemistry and molecular biology major who maintains a 3.9 GPA and is starting a summer research fellowship at UCSF. I've done a lot of sports and hobbies growing up. Despite always being overweight, I did karate, basketball, and a little soccer. I also played the violin for a few years. Due to my hormones and my weight, I couldn't do anything physical without having to lay down for hours. I mostly focused on choir in high school since it was something that I didn't have to move to do. However, in order to be seen as a productive member of the community, I got into meditation and become a clinical intern at a nonprofit community acupuncture and massage clinic. Since I found something that I actually loved doing, I was no longer depressed. I felt in touch with the environment again. Acupuncture also helped me lose weight and I can finally use my legs and do anything I want to.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I’m an 18 year old man. I grew up in a very religious home where I learned a lot about life in general. In the end it didn’t work out because of various reasons. My parents broke up. My dad got mad at God and God angry. My dad and I barely talked for a long time. My mom raised me mostly by herself. I loved football but that got suspended due to religious conflicts. My faith has been through many ups and downs. I never feel good enough for anyone. Music helps me feel more positive. I had many hardships in life so far, but that doesn’t mean I can’t overcome them. I’m so proud I got to graduate.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 21 years old and originally from California, USA. I grew up there and I went to High School . My grandparents immigrated to America from Ireland when they were very young and my family still lived there. When I turned 18 I wanted to visit Ireland so I booked a trip there and I really enjoyed it, it was a dream come true for me. I realized that I am glad to be American, I like what America is, and I’m grateful for all the opportunities here and I would like to stay here.

I do have a political opinion, I believe in Freedom. In Ireland, they are debating about whether or not to allow gay marriage. I believe that gay marriage should be allowed. I believe in a level playing field. If you get taxed, if you earn over a certain amount, you should pay. I believe in supporting your family. A family is a family, they are tied for life. This is a good thing. It is nice to be from a country which has political freedom because you can disagree and still be friends. Many people want to move here from all over the world, such as India, they want to work here in the States. In the past, Ireland was very poor and many people had no job, and if they did work they were always at risk of losing their job. I want to stay in the U.S. because it is a nice place.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am a 29 year old male, originally from a small town in Wisconsin. I am very family oriented. I have 4 sisters, 3 brothers, and a nephew that I would do anything for. I am the first person in my family to attend college, so that makes me extremely proud. I have a passion for giving back to the community and I try to do as much of that as possible. I also love animals. I have 2 dogs, 3 cats, and 5 fish. I love the ocean and anything that has to do with the ocean. I would live on the beach if I could.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I'm a 21-year old young woman from the United States. I grew up in Oklahoma, just north of Texas. My upbringing in a rural area greatly impacted who I have become. Growing up in rural America, there is a certain sense of community and understanding that you are part of something bigger than yourself. I learned the value of hard work, generosity and kindness, and how to take care of others. I learned that I am responsible for my own actions and must answer for them. I learned to be responsible with my own financial well-being and conduct business with integrity. My upbringing has made me into a much more confident, sincere, and independent person. These qualities are important as a soon-to-be college graduate with a degree in hand. I know that I'm capable of great success.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: My name is Ryan Henry. I’m 24 years old. I was born and raised in San Francisco. I enjoy spending time with family, eating out, watching movies, riding the bus or train, playing video games, listening to music, writing poems and some script writing. I grew up in the city which was a very diverse and accepting place. My parents divorced when I was very young so I spent a lot of time with my grandparents and great aunts and uncles. Many of them were women so they raised me to be a man. Men were strong and heard no matter what and women had soft hearts that spoke volumes with no words spoken. My mom is a strong individual and never settles for anything short of perfection, she expects you to work hard and put in effort to be the best that you can be. My biological father was in prison by the time I turned one. I never met him and this has greatly affected my life by causing resentment and disappointment as he isn’t present in my life. My mother couldn’t be more different from him. He never showed interest in getting to know me and lives in a different state where I’ve been unable to see him since I was 9 years old. I was fortunate enough to have an amazing mother who has always been an amazing example of what I should look for in a woman. I have a twin sister and two older brothers who I am extremely close to.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 28 years old. I am from Malta, and while I was born there, I was raised in the UK as an expat. My parents did not want my childhood to be defined by poverty. When I reached the age of 18, I felt that it was time to go back to Malta to find myself. In Malta, I worked odd jobs for a few years (factory, event, customer service jobs) to make a bit of money and then went back to UK for a University course. It was my ambition at the time to go to Asia for my masters, but due to a few personal set-backs, I did not get the grades needed for the UK University of my choice. In 2017, I flew to USA with no career or money to my name. I did not even have a visa at the time. As soon as I arrived, I was detained at Immigration and ended up locked up in a detention facility for over a year. That changed my outlook on life completely. I learned the value of freedom. Now, I am determined to never squander my freedom ever again. I started working to better myself. I studied to get my bachelor's degree in Criminal Justice. I trained to become a Certified Brain Injury Specialist. I worked for a Marketing and Event Management Company for over a year before I joined The Juvenile Justice Association, whose values and ethics I still believe in. While I was working with them, I met my current employer, who was their senior counsel. He trained me in both advocacy and law. I worked at his firm for over 2 years before he left the state and moved to Florida to focus on his health. This firm currently focuses on recruiting attorneys. When I heard about the Assistant Branch Manager role, I decided to apply because it combines everything I learned in law school with my current experience. My long-term goal is to be a civil rights attorney fighting for justice.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 20 years old. I grew up in Washington State. My family is active in the marine industry and I was taught that everything depends on the customer. We help boat people repair their boats and buy new or used ones. Through this experience and my dad's sport fishing history, I came to understand that we are only as good as our last success.

My grandfather was my hero growing up. One of the most notable events was when I got in trouble with him. My cousins, sister and I found an old paint sprayer in the barn and spray painted the side of our barn black. When I told him, he took me and my siblings to the barn to show him what we did. We were upset because he didn't punish us, so he gave us all wooden paddles. When we asked him what he was going to do, he said, "well I can't punish you, so I can't take your paddles either, so you will get a lot more pain out of these." From that day forward, I have strived to get people their things as quickly as possible and they will be happy, because I know that he would do the same.

Family, friends and my faith define me. My mother has taught me that no matter what goes wrong, everything can work out fine. My father is a very successful business man, so he taught me that nothing will work if you are not the customer's top priority. My father is my hero because he is one of the most successful in his industry. My sister is my main friend and I help her in any way I can.

My faith in God is very important to me. I don't know if I will ever be able to repay Him for what He has done in my life, but I owe Him everything that I do.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I was born in Charlottesville, VA and was brought up a Methodist. My mother was Catholic, but converted due to marrying my father. My family went to Trinity Church, but we never really spoke about God after the conversion. I grew up just like any other child. I went to Boys’ School. My mother died of cancer when I was fifteen. After her death, I became depressed. I began drinking to cope with my emotions. It was the music and literature of John Milton and others from the Romantic Age that pulled me out of that funk, and I soon lost my initial faith in Christ. By the time I was in my twenties, I developed a curiosity about eastern thought. I delved into philosophy, and read of the Church Fathers. I began writing down my ideas on the nature of God, the universe, morality, and the human condition. When my interest and ability in all things spiritual began to wane, my interest in writing began to increase exponentially. I wrote poetry and fiction, in which I described intense emotional landscapes, where God, my own mind, and other topics of great power were often explored with impressive frankness. When I joined the military, I signed a contract for a five-year term, and in return the government offered me a hefty sum of money. While on deployment to Iraq, I was injured, and received several medals for my services. After Iraq, I returned home, but have not seen combat since then. My life has become somewhat placid. I meet people, but am not a social person by nature. I am an introvert, who sees the beauty in nature, and tries his best to live a virtuous life. I just have little desire to see what is there for people to see in the world.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am a 25 year old female who grew up in Michigan. My parents had me out of wedlock and my father was an alcoholic, heavy smoker and unemployed for a period of time. I was looked after by my sister and she helped me with my school, sometimes staying with me in my mother's house when my father was drunk, or even coming to school if I couldn't be collected. This isn't a problem for me, however my mother occasionally shows me some hostility towards her previous behavior. The second part of my family lived in Hungary. My great grandfather survived the holocaust in a Hungarian concentration camp and married a woman of African descent who also survived. They moved to Michigan and were part of the local basketball community and were very kind to me, as well as affectionate, and helped me through some tough times. But I am not Hungarian, nor did my great grandmother look like a woman of African descent, both of which led me to understand that I am not full Hungarian and am racially divided. This feeling lead to a tough experience in high school and made me take no interest in learning Hungarian. I decided to go back to London when I was 15 to get away from my mother and went to live with an uncle. My uncle died 4 years ago, but my aunt is there and took me in. I was studying at college in London and I tried to get into university, but decided against it as I don't need a degree to be successful.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 18 years old. I grew up in Upland and play sports competitively throughout high school and now college. Being a competitive wrestler in the area has been one of my life goals for a long time. I am an eligible for election in 2018 as you may be able to find out when you look up Election 2018.

Also interested in other political positions soon to become available. My interests are as follows; sports, health, family, fitness, travel, working hard, volunteer work, making a difference, voter base, current and future events, watching the government, the economy and overall being conscious of what is going on.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am a Caucasian female, born and raised in the state of North Carolina. I grew up very sheltered as my mother has controlled my environment up until this point. I am 19 years old. I have two brothers and one sister. The only excitement my life held, until recently, was my pets. I had two dogs and a cat that I loved with all my heart, and they were all I was allowed to have to give me someone to love. My mother used to raise and sell exotic birds, which is why my pet collection was small. I was not aware of the world outside my home until my social security card came in the mail. It was like unlocking a whole new world. It opened up a whole new world of knowledge to me. I took courses on computers and Microsoft office. I enjoyed each class and am always interested in exploring technology. I also read in my spare time and am a quiet, but outgoing person when it comes to meeting new people. My biggest joy in life is my pets. I do not care for girly, materialistic things, but I care very much about the animals I have and hope to obtain a job that allows me to be as close to them as possible. I still live at home with my mother and attend school part-time online to work on my college degree. I have had a few jobs and live a very simple, but average life up until this point.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
“I am 20 years old and have been living in Alabama my whole life. I grew up in Opelika. I have a large family, mostly siblings, so my life was a pretty crazy growing up.”

“I chose to pursue nursing because it has always intrigued me. Being able to assist others and become a great asset to the health care system is very important to me. My father has always been in the military, so I have always had a large passion for taking care of others. I knew nursing was the path I wanted to take. Ever since I was a little girl, I have always wanted to be a doctor or work in some part of the medical field. It has always been the passion that has been in me that has helped drive me to do this. I did a lot of shadowing and volunteering at the local hospital while I was in high school. It was these experiences that led me to become even more curious and interested in this profession. It was my experiences while shadowing and volunteering at the local hospital that solidified my decision to become a nurse.”

“The best thing about being a nurse is being able to help others in their time of need.”

“I love learning and I like to push myself. Nursing seems to be a field that will continuously help you to learn and grow.”

“Ever since I have been alive I have never been someone who can sit and do the same thing over and over again. My mother put me in beauty school at a young age and I had the time of my life. I got to work with colors and hair and makeup which was my biggest passion in life. I loved getting to know different people and having them ask for my opinion on the styles they wanted. This gave me a great foundation for my working career and what I am now.”

“Each day at work I do not know what is going to happen. Each day is different and I get the chance to make people smile. I meet new people from all over the world and can always find new information that I take home with me to learn.”

“Most people think I do my job to see models and celebrities. While this is true I am lucky to work in an environment where I can meet so many different types of people.”

“Out of all my experiences working in the field of medicine, the one that sticks out the most to me is when I had a procedure done myself and the way the doctors treated me and made me feel. I had a great experience and know that I will one day do the same for patients in my care.”

“If I could give one piece of advice to someone who is interested in entering this field, it would be to take the time to research. Make sure that this is a good fit for you. Don’t be afraid to take the time to shadow others in this field. This will help you determine if this is the right career choice for you.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a 28 year old man. I grew up in the Bay Area, California. I was raised by my two loving parents in a large suburban home. Throughout my childhood I played sports and spent time with my friends. While I enjoyed school, I was never top of the class. Instead, I was happy enough to go through the steps and graduate, rarely exceeding the baseline standards. Though from an early age I dreamed about becoming a storyteller, I felt that one day I would find a real job. As I got older, my confidence in my ability increased, allowing me to confidently pursue my dreams. But still, real life seemed to give me a nudge.

As an adult, I quickly got caught up in the business world. Working for the right companies, I moved up the ladder quickly. Many within my circle dreamed of the same career path as I. Though none of us dreamed of the boredom and tedious nature of the work. Inevitably, we’d find ourselves wondering what else was out there. I found myself at a large global company, a top place to work in the world, doing something I never imagined, selling large contracts to big businesses. It was in this role that I was introduced to the world of sales, and what I was doing was the worst case of that: billing by the hour. It was with this realization I decided to fully pursue my storytelling and writing abilities. Since then I have attended numerous comedy shows, I’m about to release my first poetry collection, and I write in my spare time.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m a 26-year-old white female of Irish heritage. I grew up in a country called Canada (which is in North America).

Well, I enjoy giving, making others happy, making people smile, and showing compassion. I don’t like to think of myself as a role model for anyone, but the true role model in my life (what little time I spent with him) is my grandfather. He always gave with a smile on his face, even as I can remember. He worked with his hands, and created beautiful pieces of art. He was known to give gifts to those in need even when it would benefit him more to keep it himself. He’s what I’d consider a true gentleman, and although he passed when I was 13 I remember the things he taught me the most.

The things I enjoy are giving; doing anything that will help improve someone else’s day is something that will make mine. I love being able to surprise someone with something beautiful, funny, or just out of the ordinary, especially when it comes to gifts or something that they will remember.

I’m a person who loves helping others to better themselves and their lives. I would love to open my own daycare someday, and allow children to experience life with hands on learning experiences and kindness from friends. I was raised by a single mother who worked her butt off to give my brother and I the life we deserved. I want to do the same.

And my last answer would be this: We are all put in this world to make the best of what we have. I’m not afraid to admit that I may be sad about life from time to time, but I also believe that everything happens for a reason. It’s hard to find the light in the dark sometimes, but with the love of your family and friends you will always find that brightness somewhere. If you need me to know more, please let me know. I’ve missed talking with you, and I do plan on keeping in touch more in the future.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 25, live in West Michigan and grew up in the suburbs. I moved to Michigan for college. I wanted to be a doctor until I learned that I was very sensitive to blood and probably don't want to handle that for a living, so I wanted to help people instead and pursued psychology.

I like playing video games and going out to do things in the public. I enjoy going for coffee or going to the mall. I'm more of an extravert than an introvert, so I want to be where the action is, and I try to brighten the day of everyone I meet. I am shy at first until I get to know you, then I like to have fun and joke around. I am the comedic relief in my family, so I like to have a good laugh and get enjoyment from making others laugh, too. Life can be tough, so I like to make things lighter than they really are and find ways to cope and have fun doing so.

I care about what happens to others, since I see suffering all the time, and I want to help them because I know what it is like and I want to end that suffering. I like to see the good in people and in life, so I look for that instead of a more negative approach. I would like a job that helps people in their lowest points of their life and that provides a positive environment.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 21 years old. I am from a small town in Virginia called Kingsport. I am the 3rd oldest out of five children. I have three sisters and one brother. I grew up with more girl than boys, which was difficult for me. On the brighter side, I can hold my own on the playing field because the girls in my family like to keep up on physical activity. I spent a lot of time with my parents and siblings. My grandmother lived next door to us until she passed away of natural causes at the age of 95. That was one of the happiest occasions of my life because of the impact that grandmother had on my life. In my opinion, she was the most intelligent and influential person in my life. Being the oldest, I had to help my parents with my four younger siblings, especially my brother. I am constantly trying to help out my family because of the experiences that I have been through. I spent three years of my life at Virginia Polytechnic Institute and State University (Virginia Tech). While I was there, I started working for the campus police force. It was there that I was introduced to law enforcement. For me it was love at first sight. It was something about the power that the police have to bring criminals to justice that I found very attractive. While I was working, I was also a full-time student. I was working on a business degree in accounting and a minor in computer science. After three years, I grew tired of Virginia Tech and the old life I had. I moved to Arizona so that I can start over. It has not been easy since I have moved, but I have found a niche in the computer field, which has allowed me to make a decent living. I also found out that I loved law enforcement and wanted to find a position in my dream career. I spent a year in Arizona before I decided that my dream career was actually where I was already located, the campus police force at Virginia Tech.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 23 years old. I grew up in a small town in the state of Indiana. The entire town had a population of 1,000. I was born in a public hospital in town. My parents were married at the time. My mother’s name is Carmen Suarez and my father’s name is Johnny Suarez. My family owned a restaurant for many years. I grew up in the back of the restaurant. When I was four years old, the restaurant was closed due to a foodborne illness that killed seven people. My family was pushed out of the community and lost everything. My family is still poor. We lived in a housing project at first, but we moved out into my cousin’s garage. My cousin lived in South Florida. Our lives are filled with drama. We have a lot of problems. I am filled with a lot of rage. I was very depressed and wanted to die. I wanted to kill myself because I had no hope. I felt lost and hopeless. I felt like I had no purpose in life. My parents were very stressed and did not have time to help me. I had to help myself. I learned to help myself and to create my own happiness. I started reading books to learn how to cope with my problems. I like to read books by Napoleon Hill, Tony Robbins and Jack Canfield. My friends say that I am a strong-willed person with a strong personality. They say that I am strong enough to help myself.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 28 years old. I was born in an affluent neighborhood in Baku, Azerbaijan. My father was a successful merchant and was well-known. I attended a private school until the first years of college. I was a good student and wanted to learn everything. Many people told me, that because I had special family connections I can go anywhere I want, but in my heart, I wanted to be a physician. I earned good grades in college but later dropped out because I had a conflict with my father, who wanted me to take over the family business. He couldn’t accept my desire to help people as a physician. After two years, I got a job as an IT technician and administrator. I work in a private company in a help desk system. I started to work at this company after I left my job with EY. Working for a profit organization was not something I wanted for my career and I enjoy working for a charitable cause much more. My parents wanted me to marry a wealthy man so that my financial status wouldn’t be poor. This came from an idealistic vision of how our family wanted to be perceived, but I resisted it. I believed a human being with dignity doesn’t need to make decisions based on finance and having a special family status. I married a man I loved and didn’t give into the pressure to find a man with a higher status or someone “rich”. It is a fight every day to keep my family together. My partner and I have been living together for several years now, but our economic situation is very precarious. I am not dependent on my partner’s money and I work 10 hours a day in order to help provide for my family and to keep us from going broke. To help my son have a decent education I can’t take a break from my job. I do everything I can to make my son’s life easier, but there are not many options for parents who can’t afford to have someone watch their child while they are at work. I don’t know what the future holds for us, but I am trying to do everything I can to change our situation.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I'm a 23 year old guy born in New York (which is an unusual place to be born, haha).  I grew up on Long Island,  about an hour outside of NYC.  As a child, I've loved video games and books, namely fantasy and sci-fi, even since I was a young child.  Throughout high school I had poor grades but always had ideas for fantasy worlds in my head.  I met my first girlfriend in high school, and this led to me realizing that I was gay at the age of 17, and from there I realized that I was also pretty geeky and weird.  I loved things like books and video games and nerdy things like Star Wars and LOTR.  I was ashamed of these things and never shared them with my peers, but I made friends who accepted me for me.  After high school, I enrolled in college, with a major in pre-med.  I had high hopes of being the next Ron Paul in the White House or something, but that led to me having relatively low grades.  After some time in college, I realized my love for other things, like video games and anime.  So, I decided to drop pre-med and try and get an English degree.  This will be my third semester in college, and this last one will be the first semester in which I take all writing classes, including creative writing and a capstone course in English.  So, I guess you could say I'm still kind of evolving.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I'm 27 and live in San Francisco. I grew up in New York City and went to college in Boston. I moved to San Francisco in 2014 and now work at a startup as a website product designer. I spend my free time writing short stories, working on travel videos, biking around San Francisco, and eating burgers.

Note: Hiring managers (in any industry) will look at your Facebook account or other social profiles to see who you are. I think that a candid and detailed answer, though a little scary, is the best option here. A generic canned answer like "I've been around the world and back!" will have hiring managers skeptical that you can really do what you say.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 25 years old, have recently finished university and graduated in marketing, advertising and sales management. I am living in London. In my spare time I love to cook for friends and family. I can spend hours in the kitchen preparing a feast and while I’m cooking I love listening to music.

My ambition is to one day own my own small business in the food industry.

For the time being, I am working for an events company in London.

I come from a very close-knit family, we spend a lot of time together doing outdoor activities. I enjoy anything that gets me outdoors in fresh air.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a 24 year old white male. I grew up in the US. My biological parents were an alcoholic mother, a deadbeat father and an elder brother who was a drug addict. I grew up surrounded by domestic violence and often my mother took out her pain on my sibling and I. I also grew up in poverty and my mother rarely worked. Due to the nature of my family the only people I ever had and was close to were my two younger brothers who I cared for. We had a very close bond growing up, so when my mother and father got a divorce it was very hard on all of us. Our family was broken up and two of us were sent to live with our grand parents and the other two lived with my mothers brother.

I spent a lot of time working jobs while still living with my grandparents to support myself. I did not want to live with my uncle because he was a drug addict who frequently abused my grandmother. At the age of 17 I began working full time and dropped out of school. I was mostly responsible for myself and often had to buy groceries and pay bills on my own.

Growing up like this has made me a very hard worker. It has also helped me learn how to get along with people and has given me a pretty open minded view on life. I have no preconceived notions about people. If you treat people with respect they will generally treat you the same way.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
Again, I’m 28, born and raised in New Jersey. Grew up as the oldest child in a pretty normal Jewish-American household (mom worked, dad worked… they were reasonably involved parents… but I also got a ton of unstructured playtime). Went to a pretty good school (no pressure to do extracurriculars or get A’s on standardized testing or anything like that). I don’t know how much of that is really what made me who I am. I was pretty introverted. I read A LOT. Started working on cars for fun at age 12 and did a ton of DIY because it was cheap and fun. In college I started to get involved with computers (which quickly turned into hardware when I got an internship at Intel). After graduating I started a web dev firm, which led me to forming an actual business as I worked with VC’s, partners, employees, etc… I’ve done venture-backed startups and founded a tech firm for a long time. I’ve done some angel investing and supported other entrepreneurs.

I hate material possessions (which bothers some people more than it should). I value freedom above everything else.

I don’t really know how to write this without sounding arrogant, but my political leanings were influenced by events in my life and quite a bit of time reading and researching. I was pretty idealistic when I was younger, wanting to be an engineer, then an entrepreneur to help solve problems. I’m still a bit idealistic, but I see more of the big picture now. (https://twitter.com/vicborisov/status/332923699624841088)

I’m excited about emerging AI and its intersection with everything else.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 29 years old, and I was born and raised in Madison, WI. I’ve never lived anywhere else! A few of the key events that shaped my life would be growing up the way I did, not only in Madison but with my parents as well. I have a family of five kids, my parents, and one “bonus brother” from my parents’ marriage before they had my oldest sister. My parents have always worked hard to maintain stability within the home and have continued to show love to their children despite facing some challenges with the two youngest. My parents were never religious growing up, and although I grew up in a school with several Christian students and several Christian friends, it was always “assumed” that you were Christian and you had faith in God. My dad was diagnosed with a life-threatening brain tumor when I was in the sixth grade. This brought our family to have a deeper understanding of God and Jesus, and God has shown Himself strong through that. I also credit my high school friend group for showing me what it meant to have faith in Christ. Through their example, I decided to go to college at UW-Whitewater (go Warhawks!) and double major in Music Performance and Spanish. While I was there, I joined Chi Alpha, a campus ministry. I went on several summer mission trips where I fell in love with the heart of God for the lost, and was given a greater desire to fulfill the Great Commission. God also placed a strong desire in me to be in missions at some point in my life, but I also knew I would need a stable job that would support my family. Since graduating college, I have been playing the piano professionally. This year I got married, and my husband and I moved to Plano, TX. I look forward to fulfilling the great commission of reaching the world with the gospel, however God may call me!

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
My name is [X] and I’m 23 years old. I grew up in the town of [Y]. My parents worked hard and instilled a great work ethic in me. I have been fortunate enough to have experienced a lot of wonderful things that a lot of people never get to. I have been on a cruise to Alaska, visited Hawaii, swam with dolphins, saw the Atlantic Ocean and the Pacific Ocean, and I even got to meet a few celebrities. I’m grateful to be alive.

I started going to college at [Z] where I studied accounting and biology. In 2012, I had an internship with [A] that turned into a full-time job. After a year and a half with them, I left to pursue a career in software. I recently completed a 9-month web development program at [X].

I think I would be a good fit at your company because I have a great work ethic and I like to take on new challenges.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a white 25 year old Male, born in and from upstate New York. My formative years were in Upstate New York and Connecticut, I was the first male in my family to pursue and attain a Bachelor's Degree in Biology and Environmental Studies, graduating at the top of my class while attending Champlain College in Vermont. I have a passion for the environment, for science, for the preservation and enjoyment of natural resources, but I'm now driven by the idea of making a difference; if I really need to boil it down. Making a difference in a chaotic world. Changing things I could be seen as having a hand in. Making a positive difference in the world. Opening up opportunity for others, opening up people's eyes to an idea that its okay to improve things. Changing perceptions and using science to help reach targets. Communicating and spreading the word.

I have a passion for a lot of things, that I'm not sure if I can say enough about myself in an interview situation. I see myself as a peaceful person, who's in a state of constant reflection. I like to learn, to experience, to experience new perspectives, and to try to engage in dialogue with people I may disagree with (I find that is almost always the case). My actions or even reaction are, to me, evidence of the brain and thought that occurs to produce such a result. So it's always fascinating to me.

To summarize: I am driven by what is right, and motivated to work through any method or methods and resources to do so. From my studies I have a background in Biology, a specific focus on Environmental Science, several minors in Philosophy, Ethics, Mathematics, and others, and a working background in Philosophy, Biology, and Environmental Science. I have a dream and a vision of a better life, not just for me, but for everyone I can help to have.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 23 years old. I grew up in a small town in rural Pennsylvania. From an early age I knew I was different. By 6th grade I was already wearing a different style of clothing. In high school people were making fun of me for my fashion sense. My tastes were also very adult. I was watching murder and torture movies that were very adult and even some educational content that was out of my reach. By high school I dropped out and later on my parents got full custody of me after a stint of homelessness. I couldn’t find a job. I was playing games and reading for most of the day. I haven’t achieved much in life yet, but that is because I have only recently turned 23. I am graduating from USF in 2019, a goal that seemed impossible a few years ago. I haven’t achieved much yet, but I hope to change the world for the better, perhaps raise a family, and achieve whatever goals I am looking forward to at the time. Being my own person is something I value above all things. I have no idea if this is enough, but this seems to be what people are looking for. Whether they are right or wrong, it’s always good to find out who you really are and what you really believe in so you can achieve your goals in life.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m 23. I was born in Los Angeles. My father is a chemist. My mother is a pianist. Both live in California with my younger sister and my niece.

We moved a lot when I was little. Mexico, Canada and Ohio. Ohio is where I spent my most formative years. That’s where I had my first encounter with racism. We moved to Ohio when I was 6. A few years later the family relocated to Florida. I attended two high schools. The first was public, the second was private. The first was almost all black, the second was almost all white.

In high school I was a trumpet player. I played in the marching band, the symphonic band and the wind ensemble. When I wasn’t playing I was studying. I earned high school AP credits. I was the best student in my Algebra and Physics classes. I went to college on a half-scholarship for top math students from my high school. I majored in physics and computer science.

I attended an Ivy League university for an astronomy and astrophysics degree. The academic atmosphere was cutthroat and trying. I fell ill with depression and I’ve been struggling to recover for a long time. I didn’t even graduate.

In my spare time I make music. I sing and I play guitar and I DJ my own songs. I write songs and record them. I help others in need. I started a non-profit to help homeless children and perform at nursing homes, art schools, etc. I donate to worthy causes. I play video games. I develop video games. I am an IT specialist at a start-up (I did not discuss this at the interview but it’s relevant).

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am a 26 year old male, originally from NJ but have lived in Florida for most of my life. I am hardworking, dedicated, and humble individual. My parents always pushed me to do well in everything that I do in life. Growing up, both my parents were working full-time to provide a comfortable and safe life for my sisters and I. Knowing this, I always wanted to be a good role model for my sisters, and for others. This is one of the reasons why I wanted to go to college and get a degree, in hopes that I would make a difference in the lives of others, and for my parents to be proud of me. I also believe that my faith is a major contributor to who I am today. Religion and belief is one of the most important things to me and I hope to always be a humble individual.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a 22-year-old student who was born in the USA, but moved to New Zealand at age 7. I was an only child, and because of that, I have always been able to keep my thoughts and opinions to myself, only saying what I think is necessary. I’m the sort of person who enjoys learning about things and picking them apart to find out how they work. I like to know things.

In high school I spent my time studying art, taking every opportunity to go to exhibitions or even talk to the artist to see how they work. I wanted to know why they created the art they did, and what it meant to them. This desire to understand the why of things has never left me, and it still inspires me to look at the world around me.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 29 years old. I was born and raised in Los Angeles, California by my grandparents (both of my parents have passed away), and currently live in San Diego, California. I chose to study at the University of California, Irvine, where I received a bachelor’s degree in psychology and a master’s degree in human resources and employment relations. After graduating, I briefly held HR positions in the medical and technology industries. I am a certified college admissions counselor and college guidance program coordinator at an independent school in San Diego. I enjoy learning, traveling, writing, eating delicious food, and spending time with loved ones.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am a 25 year old male who has spent my life living in the rural counties of Vermont. My family and I live in small houses and work in small family farms. We strive to keep the tradition of the family farms alive. By paying homage to the past, we can better prepare ourselves for the future. My family produces milk, cheese, blueberries, corn, and potatoes. These foods are not only the joy of my work; they are also the sustenance of my family. By being out in the field all day, the freshness and flavor of these foods is experienced in its greatest form. There is nothing that I enjoy more than a morning spent picking corn or a sunny afternoon driving the tractor. The mountains, the sky, the trees, and the fields: they are all there with me on the farm. The farm means more to me than it does to some, because it is more than just a place for me, it’s a part of me.

My family runs the Dairy Research Center of Vermont. Every day, millions of foreign students are provided with their meals at the center. In exchange for this, they learn how to properly care for the land and cattle. My life revolves around farming. Most of my days are spent in the fields tilling the soil or repairing our equipment. The labors are slow, but I find them enjoyable. I prefer to fix the machines by myself, it gives me great satisfaction to get it to work once again. The cows love me too. From the day I was born to the day I die, cows will always hold a special place in my heart.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I’m a 24 year old male. I love music and especially electronics. In my spare time I love to tweak around with computers and play games. I currently live with my girlfriend and have been with her for almost 4 years now. We are very happy and plan on getting married sometime soon (don’t have a date yet). As I said I like music, which goes back 5 years or so. I used to have no friends and didn’t really have any contact with other people in school. Then I discovered something. Everyone loves music, as long as you have similar interests. So I started playing in my schools jazz band, and this gave me my first set of friends. I was accepted into the “normal” realm of teenagers and finally felt I belonged somewhere. I credit the change to my music teacher who was an incredible person. For the next two years I tried to give something back by being a tutor for her during lunch. The reason I did that was to say thank you and also to continue to be a part of the “music” club. Later I joined my high schools jazz band. I dropped out of the first one because I didn’t feel that people were truly interested. My second attempt at the jazz band was much better and I never looked back. I did about a year and a half of that and loved every moment of it. I had grown so much and become an integral part of the group.

After high school, I couldn’t just sit around and do nothing with my life. So I applied to a technical college where I now work. My goal at the time was to become a mechanical design engineer (which hasn’t changed). However I needed to learn CAD (computer aided design) so that I could design the machines I wanted to build. I attended the college for about 2 years but it just wasn’t the right fit for me. I decided to transfer to a different college that was a four year university. (Since I had already started and not finished my engineering program I would only be there for 2 years) I was a bit nervous but I thought I would enjoy it. Well I did have a good time but I had a few problems. First of all engineering did not satisfy my creative needs. Second, college is pretty expensive (Especially in Canada). Third, I didn’t want to work at a desk for 50 years. Now I’m not saying that there isn’t a way around that. All I’m saying is that it wasn’t right for me. I didn’t want to design a sprocket for a lawn mower. I wanted to build the lawn mower.

I also met my girlfriend in college and she pushed me to try something new. I had a few ideas but never really tried them because I was trying to get into engineering. One day when I was in my electronics course I was taught about the different types of oscilloscopes that we had in the school. There were 2 main types. Those of which used a CRT (cathode ray tube) and those which used LCDs (liquid crystal displays). I was very impressed by the quality and also by the pure speed of the LCD models. At the time there wasn’t one for sale (that I could afford). So I decided to try to build one.

I always wanted to be a maker and not an engineer. But all of my makerspace experiences at that point (with the exception of working on electronics and computers in my garage) had been terrible. I would go into a DIY electronics store and spend hours upon hours looking around. I would never know what to do or what to buy. It was so confusing. It wasn’t that I was so far away from the beginning. I had no idea where to even start. A gentleman was brought into the school to help all of the students understand what they would be doing for their project. One of my classmates asked him about his old oscilloscope. He was happy to explain how it works and to let him borrow it (after he explained that it wasn’t working very well). My classmate asked if he could take it home and mess around with it to see what was wrong with it and it’s capabilities. I decided I wanted to learn more about these instruments so I asked if I could have it after that. He said that it was completely broken and that I probably didn’t want to use it. I decided to take it home anyways. I found it in a drawer and decided to try to build an oscilloscope when I got home. It turns out that I had built a perfectly functional oscilloscope. I’ve added a few extra features and remodeled it a bit. The projector lamps that I was using for the light source were replaced with a led light. The only reason for that was to help it be lighter. All of the tubes and bulbs have since been removed, so it’s a whole new circuit. It now has additional inputs such as BNC and RCAs so that I can connect things much more easily. It still has quite a few bugs in it, but it’s still a very functional unit.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: First off, I am 27. I was born in Bellwood, IL. My parents still live in the same city. My youth has been full of the usual ups and downs such as good grades, making friends, being picked on and having the toughest teachers and coaches. My values came from when I was told I needed to grow up and become an adult. I remember I had my life thrown onto me right before my senior year and I figured I better get it together. I grew a career for myself as a cable installer and kept moving up in the industry. At 21 I was an instructor, then I was a trainer, and a lead in the field. Since my father was a business owner, I became interested in his kind of work, which has always been my end game. My hobbies and interests are self-education, physical health and quality time spent with my family and fiancée. I have just come out of 11 years of Catholic school so I know quite a bit, and I find that it’s useful in almost every area of life. I had lived with my fiancée for two years and my three year old daughter for a year and a half. Due to unforeseen circumstances we broke up and that led us to the realization that we would never achieve our goal of having a happy family. I’ve been financially stable since my second year of installation. I’m a very hard and dedicated worker that has the skills and experience to compete with other applicants.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a 25-year-old male. I grew up in Prescott, AZ. I was always a great student growing up and dreamed of going to MIT since my 5th grade science teacher showed us a video of a blackboard in front of a classroom of students, and when the professor erased a formula that had been written on the blackboard the students in the class, the professor, and the camera reacted to the formula and answered questions about it verbally on their own – imagine! What did that teach me? Whatever else I may accomplish in my life I had to study something that was interesting to me.

When my senior year of high school came along, and I had to chose colleges to apply to, MIT’s Calculus courses were never on the syllabus. I then heard about mechanical engineering, so I studied that, and then I heard about biotechnology engineering, so I studied that. I read and read as much as I could from textbooks all the way up to a masters level in biology. It became a means to an end. I was tired of working summers at Walmart to put myself through college and paying student loans from my part time employment, so I told my parents I could still graduate in four years, but I would also simultaneously be in a top med school program. What would that mean? I was blessed with the opportunity to study full time at MIT’s 5 year biotechnology program, apply to only about 10 med schools my final year of college, and then start med school my final year at MIT my spring quarter. Since I graduated MIT, I’ve been doing very little else than attend my PhD course at USC in molecular & cellular biology and learning medical school to pass boards and eventually get into a residency of my choice.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 29 years old. I was born in Detroit, Michigan. I grew up in the inner city which gave me a healthy dose of reality that most people don’t get. I have had two moms and two dads; one was African American the other was white. My mom is my biological mom. I never really got along with my biological dad until he was dying in 2010. My parents were good parents, but my mom was 18 and my dad was 20 when they started having kids. That, coupled with substance abuse on both their parts, made for a not so ideal home life. My parents divorced when I was 2 and then my mom married my step-dad when I was 8. He has always been my real dad and my mom’s husband. I am thankful that my step-dad was more of a father than my biological dad ever was. I am fortunate enough to have a sister and a brother. My sister is 25 and my brother is 31. I have three children of my own. Cole is 9, Eddie is 5, and Ty is 3. They are my world and I love every day with them.

In 2012, I started working at our local regional daily paper. My official title is sports features writer, but I’ve also done a little bit of everything there. Since high school, I’ve always been a go-getter, a one-upper, a do-whatever-it-takes-to-make-this-happen kind of guy. I take personal pride in my work.
