If you live in the US and aren’t familiar with it, you might associate Las Vegas with the iconic strip that includes Vegas, Caesar’s Palace, Mandalay Bay, and Planet Hollywood. What you might not be familiar with is the other side of Las Vegas that includes some of the best hiking and scenery in America.

We spent most of our time hiking around Red Rock Canyon and in the nearby Mount Charleston. When I visited Vegas, I discovered that it’s not just a place for partiers. It’s also a place of mountains, beautiful views, and hiking. Red Rock Canyon is right in the middle of Vegas and you can’t help but enjoy the view!

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: My name is Anran Shi. I am a 23 year old male and I was born on May 5th 1991 in Tianjin China. I am an only child and live in Tianjin with my parents. I lived in Tianjin my entire life until I came to California last year to finish my undergraduate degree in Electrical and Electronic Engineering. Growing up with a single child has been great, having both parents and having no siblings meant all the focus was on me. I was always provided with all the necessary support to do my best and to succeed. I have been involved in piano for 15 years starting at the age of 8. Both of my parents are music teachers at elementary schools in Tianjin. I was able to travel around with my father to various competitions around China. This was a great experience and I hope to be able to give back to the community by also joining piano competitions later. I am also able to speak German fluently as a result of my exchange programs that I have been in. I was the runner up of a regional German speaking competition in Tianjin. I graduated high school in the United States and this was one of my most rewarding experiences. I came to the United States in August of 2007. I arrived at the University of California Merced and immediately immersed myself in the college lifestyle. That same year I made the Dean’s Honor List and Graduated Magna Cum Laude in June of 2013. I joined the organizations Intramural Sports and Pi Kappa Phi that I am still involved in today. I am the Treasurer of Intramural Sports as well as the Social Media Consultant. I am also the treasurer for Pi Kappa Phi and still involved in as much as I can be because I was co-founder of the fraternity. I am currently a graduate student at California State University Long Beach.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 29 years old and I was born in New York. As a young girl, I went to school at Columbia University where I earned a degree in Architectural Design. As a child, my life was difficult. My parents were both heroin addicts, and they did not have much money. However, throughout my life I learned to value hard work. In my high school years, I earned a full scholarship to go to college, and I worked hard to continue this trend throughout my education. After I graduated from college, I moved to Manhattan where I got a job at a prestigious law firm. I have worked as an environmental lawyer for the past 5 years and have helped to obtain permits to clean up contamination sites for the EPA.

In my spare time, I enjoy spending time with my family, especially when I get to visit my grandparents in Kentucky.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
Wow, that’s a lot to answer in 1000 words, so let me think about this for a moment.

Well, I am 27. I am a native of Pittsburgh, PA. I was adopted and I was given up because I was diagnosed with ADHD and the thought was that I could be in the hospital at the time. My parents were older than most other parents, and this was common 27 years ago for parents to get a child through a pregnancy and then to adopt and to care for another child that came along. I don’t have any biological siblings, although I do have cousins that are around my age. The first ten years of my life were not easy for me. I was put in a new school, in a new house and it was very difficult to adjust to. It took me a long time to learn to talk and to walk, although my parents never lost faith in me. They gave me a lot of encouragement and despite the hardships, I never felt like they were judging me. It was more like we were partners in life and that we each had our parts to play and it all worked out the way it was supposed to be. I was blessed with wonderful parents.

When I was 10, the family moved to Ohio, where I would meet a girl that was older than me, but with whom I could talk, relate and it seemed we both had a lot in common. Her name is Jessica Marie, she was nine years old. When I got home, I asked my parents if we could adopt another child. They got to meet Jessica. They loved her and Jessica became a part of our family and like a sister to me. She taught me everything there is to know about being cool and to this day, she is the coolest woman I know. We have since gone our own ways and she is living an adult life now and we aren’t really as close as we once were, but for years we were like sisters and it was always so nice. She got me into rock and roll music, which my parents encouraged, so that was an added bonus. And it also meant I could rebel, just a little.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 25, I grew up in a small town in the Rocky Mountains. Some people in that town still live there. That’s it, we left as soon as we could. We moved to LA to start anew and join in to make an impact in a city where change was coming fast. I was born and raised in that small town, it’s where my family has lived since the Gold Rush in 1849, and I do not like to leave my comfort zone. Change is a rough taskmaster and it scares me. However, I moved to LA. There are so many different types of people here that I’ve learned so much about life, especially from the people I’m involved with. It’s rare that I talk about religion. I am religious, however, it’s no where near a priority in my life. My God is Jesus Christ and He loves all of us, no matter our circumstances. He, like all other religions, believes in absolute peace. My life has a lot of life in it. I work my ass off, I am a hustler, I have gotten little to no sleep in the past few years, but I love every bit of it. In my free time I like to fish and get drunk at home with my husband and dogs. I like to surround myself with people who are better than me and suck up every ounce of knowledge they have. I am always interested in what others are doing, how they do it and why. I have come so far from where I started and I owe a lot of it to the people I’ve encountered and learned from.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I'm 25. I grew up in San Antonio, Texas, my entire life. My father was in the military and he is why I grew up here. The military also put me in the position to experience the diversity the US has to offer, while living overseas and spending my summers at my grandparents' houses in Canada and New Zealand. Having experienced all of this I've become really well-rounded and I'm open to trying anything new.

Some of the things I enjoy include soccer and cooking. I love cooking, but it isn't always easy to find the time to prepare something to eat. In addition to soccer, I try to stay active by working out, biking and running. I recently did a triathlon, and I'm thinking about running a marathon.

While these are some of my main interests, I can be adaptable. In college I discovered an interest in hiking, and as a kid I loved playing video games. I'm up for trying new things and that is why I decided to become an auditor.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: G'day mate...My name is Tim I am 28 years old and have just moved to the US to find love with a decent caring woman. I am from South Australia born and raised. I am 5ft11 and weigh a clean 215 pounds, looking for something more with a decent caring woman who can love and appreciate me for who I am. I enjoy having a drink or 2 with friends, going on walks on the beach or visiting wineries. I enjoy going on road trips to different states and cities. I am a loving honest caring person who has a lot to offer to a relationship who has been hurt in the past with people lying and cheating. Please if you would like to know more about me please contact me, I am ready and willing to meet you as well and see where it goes. But first could you please ask your dating site to verify your credit card records. This is so that I can see it is a legit site and not a scam site so that I know we are in this 100% and I will tell you what I want too so we can talk and get to know each other more. I am here to please and keep you happy and make you feel special as you deserve to be. You will not find a more caring down to earth man than me as I will treat you with the most respect.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: Ok so I'm not sure what exactly to say but I am a 19 year old citizen of the united states of America. I moved to Kansas from Uganda when I was just a little girl with my family. I remember my little sister taking the family pet and falling down the stairs, I remember us moving into a smaller house that my parents had bought and that we had to share a room together for many years, and I also remember arguing with my brother all the time (still do sometimes). It wasn't easy growing up. My parents divorced and I never saw my father or my step father since. Growing up, we were always low on money which in turn made it hard for me to make friends. I was bullied for my looks and that just made it hard for me to really trust people. I moved to Arizona in high school and found some really amazing friends who I still talk to today. It's been a rough road but I'm glad to say that I'm happy. What I really love doing is playing sports. I love being on the court playing basketball, being on the ice playing hockey, and any other chance I get to be athletic, I'll take it. I'm really passionate about the things I love. A few hobbies of mine are reading books, listening to music, and playing video games. My favorite book is to kill a mockingbird and my favorite song is To God be the Glory by newsboys.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I'm an 18 year old college student. I grew up in a very wealthy area, because my parents are business owners. I played soccer and ran track. I didn't particularly enjoy either. I was raised very well, and I enjoyed most of my childhood. My most enjoyable part of my childhood, though, was when I spent summers helping out at my dad's camp. I like to think that we are a family business, and we all care about what happens to it, and each other. I love working outside, and I enjoy just about anything related to fishing, hiking, cars, sports, and anything outdoors. I've found myself enjoying working on the camp from about the age of 5, and I continued to enjoy it until I grew into my teenage years. As I grew into my teenage years, I went to party more often, and I realized I didn't really enjoy fishing, hiking, cars, or sports. I still enjoyed some of these things, but my true interests had changed. I also made several bad decisions, which I will not mention, for obvious reasons. I learned from these bad decisions, and I currently live in Texas to get a higher level of education, while being closer to my family, as I have better relationship with my family than with my friends. Now I enjoy tinkering on cars, playing video games, and maintaining my vehicles, including cleaning. Even though I'm not as interested in fishing, hiking, cars, or sports, I still don't mind participating in them.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m 26 and my birthday is April 23rd. My mom was a single mother so I was raised by my mom and my grandma who lived with us until she moved in 2006. My grandma was an incredibly wise woman and I learned a lot from her before she passed away in 2010. My grandma did a lot for us when I was growing up. She raised me to be a good man. My grandmother was my role model and was my mentor.

My mom worked a lot so that makes it hard for us to spend time together. Even though I had the two women in my life raising me while my mom worked two jobs, I wasn’t spoiled. I’ve always worked to make my own money and I’ve never been in debt. I have three realtives that have introduced me to the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints. It began with my aunt, then my mother introduced me to my other aunt, and then my aunt’s husband showed me.

The church’s website says Jesus Christ is the centerpiece of our religion and that we follow His teachings. It was interesting to me how much emphasis the church places on its members helping people. The website had all sorts of interesting information. One thing I find interesting and relevant to our discussion is the church’s perspective on following civil authority. I’m also interested in why you wrote your message to me the way you did.

“Lincoln, I want you to know that I don’t think you’re a vile, moronic POS, and that goes to show how shallow people’s respect for the law really is.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
Okay, I'm 23 years old and I grow up on a small farm in Germany.

Until three years ago I worked as a sales representative for a pharmaceutical company. I am finished with high school and studying medicine. I am the first of my family to attend college.

I learned that family is very important to me because I almost lost mine. My grandfather died and I had to take care of my grandmother. It was a good time to discover that my job doesn't make me happy. I now have to decide between going back to Germany or to live in Israel. That is one reason why I am interested in Israel.

My grandmother and I like to bake during the weekend. My family and I love to make fondue in the winter. I also enjoy to ride my bike and I go to the movies with my friends every chance I get. My favorite book is called "The Pianist." It is the story of a Jewish pianist. I also went to Krakow to learn about my families history. The movie was a little too difficult to watch for me.

My mother always says "everyone has their time." When my grandmother has her cancer test results, we wait in the waiting room and sit and joke and read or knit. I think all of these little things made me who I am. I could go on and on with more, but now I think I will stop and let you ask your questions.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am male, 22 years old, I am from a middle class working family in the American Mid-west. I spent most of my life in a small town that I loved. I love to write, play video games, listen to music and hang out with friends. I grew up with books and intellectual ideas, and loved science from an early age. I first learned about Yales reputation when I got curious about the ranks of universities after graduating high school. This triggered an interest in Yale, the Ivy League and similar programs all over the country. I became obsessed with the ideal of higher education, but also saw it as a way of escaping the small town life I had. I did everything I could to go to college, and unfortunately that meant leaving everything behind in the process. It is the first time in my life that I was able to truly express myself the way I wanted, and it was the first time I was truly happy.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I'm 24, and was born and raised in a small farming community in Utah.

I really enjoy fishing. I can tell you the history and lore of fish in every major river in the contiguous US, and the techniques needed to catch them.

I eat soy sauce on every single thing that I eat, unless it’s a dessert.

I’m a voracious reader. My parents read to my brother and me every night from as soon as we could pay attention to story until I was about 12. We read our way through the Chronicles of Narnia, the Earthsea trilogy, The Silmarillion, and countless other works. To this day, I can still recite each of the seven planets of our solar system's 500-digit prime factorization off by heart. I’m currently reading Thomas Hobbes’ Leviathan.

I graduated from high school with top honors and was quickly accepted to the most prestigious university in my state. During my freshman year, I joined a nonprofit that taught women in refugee camps abroad how to build a life for themselves in a new country. During my spring break that semester, I traveled overseas and stayed with a family of my "students" to see their new lives in America. I returned home after spending a few months abroad and then went back to school. Since then, I have worked extensively with this organization to help it grow from a brand new nonprofit to a nationally recognized, highly prestigious organization, which has helped to advocate for the rights of marginalized women and their children in over a dozen countries across the globe. I’ve given keynote speeches at over a dozen major conferences in my field of study, including the TED stage. I’ve won five national book awards and been featured by several top-tier media outlets. I live in a tiny house on the side of a mountain in a semi-remote part of the country, so I have a 6-speed motorcycle to commute in. Despite being twice as old as you are and having considerably more experience in life, I promise that I can provide you with all the time and effort necessary to make a meaningful connection with your writing.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I lived most of my life in Queens, New York. I moved to Sacramento about a month ago. My adult life has been mostly dictated by economic constraints. I started working in my early teens and it was not at all rewarding. I lived with my family and then moved away to live with my girlfriend for two years. We could not afford to stay in the area, so I moved in with my dad again. One and half years ago, I was laid off from an engineering consulting firm. My last step in my life as an "earner" was making me feel like a failure. My search for work began with the realization that I will have to work at a pay check job that I would hate or get into something new. I wanted to pick something that would give me a "feeling" that I am doing some good in this world. I have many interests, many of which are physics, astronomy, philosophy, psychedelics, permaculture, etc. I am a learner and a professional tinkerer (in that order). I would have liked to learn more about computers and electronics, but I had no outlets for that type of self education. When the teaching position became available at the local New Frontier High School, I jumped on it. To be successful, you must not only be passionate about what you do, but you must love every moment of it. I love teaching. I love introducing a completely different idea to kids every day and watching them smile as they understand it.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 18 years old, grew up in Phoenix, Arizona. My parents bought our house when I was one year old and both of them still live there, my step-dad works at the school district and my mom is a veterinarian. I loved it, we lived in a relatively new housing development and all the kids I grew up with in the neighborhood went to the same schools with me. I was born in April and my dad worked at that school as a custodian, and my mom was a vet technician. When I was two, my parents split up and my mom moved in with my grandma. My grandma is also a vet technician, so I grew up around a lot of animals. She worked mostly at dogs at her office, and I grew up around cats and dogs. My mom was engaged to a new man and was happy, so after I graduated high school I moved with them and their kids (I'm the oldest of 6 kids) into their house. I lived with them for a year and a half, and then moved into my own apartment. I have a dog named Flash and two cats named Miss Kitty and Romulacat and I just graduated in December. I spent all my senior year of high school preparing for the SAT and then applying to colleges and now I'm a freshman at the University of Arizona. I would like to major in Communications because it's easy and it pays well. Nothing really matters to me except money, there's nothing else I can see myself doing. I hope to move to San Diego or Orange County and get a job there after I graduate.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am 25 years old and I grew up on the US East Coast. I was born in the mids of world war two. I was born on the way to the hospital and the doctor never made it there. I was in Brooklyn in a Jewish hospital. When the hospital realized that I was Jewish they refused to treat me. My father was Jewish but my mother was not. We did not know how to get my father into the hospital. I was no longer breathing. My father begged the nurses. They let him into the emergency room. They could not save my mom. The stopped trying after one hour when they realized I was still alive. The nurses returned to their rooms and did not touch me for a week. The hospital evacuated the children’s wing to go to another hospital. They kept me in a morgue. It is close to a cemetery there. My grandparents on my father’s side got me out of the hospital. It was illegal for a Jewish child to be without parents. My grandmother in a suit went in as my mother. She could not look like a Jew. My grandfahter took me in the other door. We were able to steal out and were saved. There were piles of bodies. My father came to pick us up from the morgue. They did not know he was Jewish until after my mom had died. He asked the doctors for my body but they would not give it to him. They wanted to keep my mom’s body at a certain temperature. I had to be at the same temperture. If it was any warmer I would have died. They killed one hundred of the babies. If they were Jewish they did not get buried or cremated. They wanted to cut down on the population of the Jews. It was called burning baby syndrome. There is a memorial for those babies that were lost in the morgue today.

After the war, my dad took me back to Poland. We went to the Gestapo House. It was still standing. It was the station where my dad had been arrested by the Gestapo for speaking out against the Germans. As he came out of the building there was a man there with a stack of papers. My dad put a fistful of papers in his hand. He asked him for proof and a picture and his story. They changed their names because the name of their father could get them in trouble. My grandparent’s numbers are on the memorial in Poland for children who were lost. I still go back. It is my home. I lived with my grandparents there. They wanted me to be raised with a strong Jewish identity but I wanted to be a part of my community. It was the sweetest, most interesting childhood I could ask for.

My grandfather was funny and smart. He was a businessman and a warrior. He was shot 33 times in the war. He had over fifty operations. He walked with a cane but he was not afraid to take on anyone. If we walked past Germans he would yell to them. He would say, they get to live but we don’t? He was proud of my grandmother. My grandmother was a beautiful lady, her name is Valerie. She was raised by the Nazis. She had stars on her clothing and she could work the system. She sent money back to her family. She left her house and village and was never allowed to come back. She spoke up for children and was going to school to get her nursing degree when the war started. The Nazis gave her a job with the children so she could leave the village. She was as smart as a whip. She was powerful. The Germans were scared of her.

When we left Poland, we moved back to the US. We lived in a small town in Florida. We lived in the trailer park in a small mobile home. Our neighbors were Irish and black and Jewish. My grandmother’s father got a lot of money and he gave my dad an education. He gave me a gift of education. I went to public school and private Jewish school. My dad sacrificed for us to have a Jewish education and he built my library for us. That was the best gift ever. He got what he wanted to do and the things that did not work out. He worked in a textile plant. He quit. He did not need to work there. He wanted to be on his own. He wanted to work for himself. He opened his own store. It was a 10 by 20 house. He sold vacuum cleaners. It was a hard life but he did everything on his own. He was an inspiration and made us live our dreams. We got to write the story of our lives. He taught me to be a leader not a follower. We had and still have a lot of love in our family. The people who read his stories are impacted by it. It is the experience of our lives.

We moved to Westchester, New York and we did not know a single soul. My dad made us start a new chapter in our lives. He gave us the spark to do the best we could in our lives. He was there to help us get what we wanted. We had his guidance and support. We had a neighborhood to help us. We were fortunate to have a community that we fit in to. The Jewish community has given me a lot of love. My father is looking up at me from heaven and smiling. He gave us the love to do our best. He was there to guide us and help us do our best. We live for him. He did not want us to believe in our parents when they said we could not do something. He wanted us to push back and say what could I do to change that. He taught us to live for us and not for others. We are the story tellers of our lives. We are the master of the journey.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am a 23 year old male. I grew up in Rocklin, CA. I was never pushed by my parents to be better or better something. When I was young I would swing on a rope in my garage for hours trying to be the fastest spiderman, and when I was in school I’d stay up late reading comics and science books because I was fascinated by the world around me. My main drive to be better is seeing something and having that desire to learn it and master it. To be the first at something is something I always seek to be, as it’s part of what made me good in school and gets me to better myself.

In recent years I have reached more and more into academics and self-improvement. I am always looking to improve on myself and further my knowledge of everything there is. I make up hobbies and interests to learn more about. I enjoy to learn just about anything, currently I am learning Spanish. I put 20 hours a week into it.

I wake up every morning at 4:30. I am at my 9:00 AM class by 7:50. I study my Spanish from 8:30 to 9:00. I go to class until 11:00. I then have class again from 2:00 until 4:20. I then go to my Spanish lab which goes from 5:00 until 6:20. And finally I have Spanish class from 7:00 to 9:00. I then come back home and do homework until about 10:30 or 11:00. And then I’m up again at 4:30.

The events that have lead me to be who I am today are my experiences in high school, my friends, my education, my curiosity, and my studies into Spanish.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I’m in my late twenties. I grew up in a pretty typical environment for the area. Most of my accomplishments thus far have been on the academic side. I don’t have any great qualities about me that I can name and I don’t find myself being as outgoing as a lot of other people are, even though it may seem that way at first. Besides academia, I have a handful of other interests that I pursue in my time. Most of the things that really matter to me revolve around religion, though. That’s the one main thing that I feel strongly about, not just that it matters to me, but that I want to be involved in it.

I’m good at explaining. I like being helpful. I like learning. I don’t really like math. I like what school has to offer more than the educational process itself. I like video games. I like talking to people. I like being alone. I like reading. I have a few pets. I like camping. I like many different types of music. I like writing, whether it’s writing a book, writing a journal entry, or just writing a blog. I love hearing people’s stories and talking to new people.

In short, I am a person who lacks clarity. I do the best I can though. Nobody’s perfect.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 21 years old. I was born and raised in North Wales, South East Asia. My early life was spent mostly at home and with my immediate family. Having gone to a good school I then moved to the UK for university. I started 2 years late (at the age of 19) and I am still studying. I am enjoying the process of self-discovery at the moment. I discovered Buddhism a few months ago and this has opened my eyes to a deeper dimension of life. The teachings on mindfulness have taught me to not lose track of who I really am, to gain a deeper understanding of the human mind. Mindfulness gives me a new-found freedom from the internal and external pressures that people face daily. I go to many talks and meditation classes to learn and to gain experience, this gives me a unique and honest insight into the workings of the human mind. I am, by nature, quite a shy, self-conscious and thoughtful person. I can also be quite emotional at times and I have problems expressing my real emotions to people (although I am trying to overcome this). This does mean that it can be hard for me to be at ease with others in the beginning, especially if I do not know them very well. Yet, once you get to know me, you will probably find me to be very caring, thoughtful, kind, selfless and loving. I love to have a bit of a laugh, socialise, go out for food/drink, travel, adventure etc. when given the opportunity.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I have lived in San Diego my entire life. I am 28, went to St. Augustine HS (SD), graduated Cal, attended classes at SDSU, got my Masters in Education from Brown University and my teaching credential at SDSU.

I was also married for a short time. My ex-wife is now dating Ben Roethlisberger of the Pittsburgh Steelers.

I live with my mother in San Diego and care for her during the day. She is from the South Side of Chicago and cooks amazing southern food. I have 3 siblings. I am the oldest, followed by my brother who was adopted from Korea, and lastly my sister who is 6 years younger than I am. Her middle name is Selena which is really annoying.

I have a quirky and wacky personality. I enjoy playing pool, listening to alternative rock, hanging out with friends, and catching some sloppily choreographed dancin’ at night clubs. I love food and try to limit my intake. Otherwise, I’ll eat burritos every day.

From time to time, I really enjoy all of the above mentioned social activities. Other times, I just want to be left alone for hours with nothing but 2 dogs, a magazine, and a tall ice cold beer.

My best friend is Brian Kelly. We have been friends since birth.

I lost my father at the age of 5. He was an alcoholic and died at the age of 39 of cirrhosis of the liver. I have been around alcohol my entire life, so I do my best to avoid it. I just don’t have a lot of fun around it.

I guess I am a pretty decent person all around. I am kind, friendly, and fair. I think that other people would say I’m smart, funny, and sarcastic (all of which I think are true). I am a true mentor, somebody who could mentor you in any way that you could possibly imagine.

So there ya have it. Weird, huh?

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m 25 and I was born in Puerto Rico. I came to USA when I was 8 years old, living in Staten Island. I became interested in the armed forces after learning about the history of Puerto Rico. The Puerto Rican independence. The army has been a part of my life growing up. I started playing soldier with my friends when I was 9. I always wanted to be part of the army and I am finally able to do that. I began my journey into the military 7 years ago. My father was in the army and now my uncle is, and my father has always told me how the army has affected their lives.

I am the oldest of 7 kids. My dad was never there and my mom was always working so I didn’t have much of a father role model. However, I wanted to be there for my siblings because I remember what it was like growing up and not having a father. From there I realized I always wanted to help others and be the leader I needed when I was younger, and the military was the perfect fit.

I have a 3 year old son who is my world. His name is Diego and he is the center of my attention. He looks up to me as a role model and hero, so I have to make sure that the example I set for him is positive. I want him to succeed in life. I want him to be a leader in whatever he sets his mind too, to become a good person, have a good heart, and be of use to his community and society.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: At the time of my interview, I was 23 years old. I was born in Atlanta, Georgia and grew up in Atlanta. I didn’t move to the state of Georgia until I was about 9 years old. I have no recollection of my life before Georgia and I don’t know anything about the area that I was born in. When I was in elementary school, I enjoyed learning new things and I was involved in the Honors program. I went to a magnet school and I was in gifted classes. As I entered middle school, I began to hate school. I began to rebel against authority and I was always getting in trouble for fighting. My parents eventually took me out of school and put me in home-schooling. They thought I was too immature to go to a regular school. I began to play online games and use the internet in general. I became very interested in computers and became a hacker. I hacked into my school’s computer system, defaced their website, and shut down their internet service. Eventually, I was able to shut down the city’s internet service by hacking into the police department’s computer system and rerouting their internet service to me. Eventually, I got caught and my parents punished me by taking away my internet. Eventually, I was able to get the internet back by calling the internet provider and convincing them to give me the internet back. My parents took away my computer for a while, but eventually I was able to get another computer and I went back to being a hacker. I hacked into various school systems, local government agencies, and businesses. Eventually, I got in trouble and I was charged with computer crimes and sent to juvenile detention. I was there for about 6 months and then I was released. I moved back in with my parents and I had to complete community service. I began to go to school and I completed my GED. I graduated high school and I began to attend college. I was studying psychology and computer science. After two years, I dropped out of college and I began working as a programmer. I enjoyed programming and I wanted to become a professional programmer. After I had been working for 2 years, I was invited to a networking event. There I met a man named Socrates. Socrates was a private investigator and he offered me a job as a PI. I was interested in PI work, so I decided to take the job. I’ve been a PI for about a year now.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I live in Colorado. I have lived here since 2005. I grew up in California. I never got into sports when I was younger so I never had the chance to play any team sports. I didn’t get to play soccer until I was 22 years old. I’m still not very good at it, but I love the game. I’ve been playing recreational soccer 3 nights a week in 2 different leagues. Currently I’m on 2 teams in the same league. I love to ride mountain bikes and go snowboarding during the winter months. I do a bit of woodworking as a hobby. I made two tables that my kids use. I enjoy reading, but I don’t have as much time as I’d like to read these days. I also like watching tv and movies. I’ve never been big on going out to eat. I’m not big on crowds and fast food places always seem to have long lines. My wife and kids like going out to eat so sometimes I go. When I was single, I never went out to eat. I’m not a big fan of sit down restaurants. I have very little experience eating at a sit down restaurant. Most of my meals are quick and cheap. My wife and kids love cheeseburgers, but I’m not a fan of cheeseburgers. I’ve only eaten a hamburger a few times in my life. I do love beer. You won’t catch me drinking very often, but if I had a chance to drink beer I’d take it. Beer is one thing that has no worries about getting old. The older the beer the better it gets. As far as food, my favorite is apples. I can eat a whole bag of apples in a sitting. I only get to eat them a few times a year, because when apples are out of season they are super expensive and not very good quality.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I’m 22 years old and I was born and raised in Orlando, Florida. My dad is Cuban-American and my mother is Puerto Rican. I didn’t have it too difficult in life, to be honest. My parents grew up tough. They both grew up in poverty, in the hood, so they had to work hard their whole lives to get where they are now and they gave me the privilege of not having to work as hard as they did to get to a decent life.

Also, I recently signed a contract with Universal Music Group, which I have been working on since the beginning of last year with my manager that I love and trust. I decided to leave New York to pursue music in L.A. for a better opportunity. I never believed in moving out here. I hated moving here. I love it now, but I hated it here. It’s been hard moving to L.A. because the only thing that made me happy in New York was the snow. I love the snow! I love the snow so much.

In five years, I see myself working on my fourth album. I always listen to my music and look at it from different perspectives. I had a thought in my head when I moved out here and that’s because I wanted to pursue a different sound. In my next album, I’ll be exploring pop music, R&B music, and urban. I don’t want to stick to just reggaeton because there’s more to music. I always expand my sound. I’ll be venturing out to the rock world, and exploring more of that. I can’t wait!

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am currently 20 years old and I live in Mount Airy, Maryland. I grew up in Mount Airy because my mother grew up here and decided she wanted to raise her children in this community, and not in the city of Baltimore where my father grew up. Even though my mother and father were not together, they still had me and my sister. Being the only child and having my parents go to school for their careers, I grew up being mature for my age and taking care of myself since I was little.

Where I grew up had a lot to do with who I have become. Living in a small town had its disadvantages and advantages. I graduated from Catoctin High School in the top 5 percent of my class. Because there were only around 200 students in my class, I never saw myself as smart nor motivated since every single person in my school was intelligent. It was not until I got to college did I actually experience what it was like to be in the classroom with people who are different. What matters to me in my personal life is my friends and family. I have many friends, and having them by my side motivates me to keep working hard and being independent. It is really cool to know I am the only one who is trying to go to college out of my group of friends so I like to get advice and give advice to those who want to be independent. I have a supportive boyfriend, who motivates me to be who I am because I do not want to live my life with regret and he wants to see me succeed in college. At the same time I do not want to work all the time and miss out on some of the fun things in life. I get home sick. I enjoy being outdoors. I like traveling and seeing new places. I love being with my dog, and I love socializing and talking to new people. I am not much of a people person, but I enjoy talking to new people as long as I know I can depend on that person. I was not always like this but I know everyone has their own differences and struggles so talking to someone new makes me realize I am not that different from anyone else.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
(Some of my answers - Not all of them)

I am 25 and I am originally from Plano, TX. My father is from Lebanon and my mother is from Lebanon. I'm the second oldest of 10 children and I love my family. My father immigrated here in the early 80's and he set up the foundation of a small company. My family lives off his income but my father loves his family and so he pushed us. He pushed us to go to college. I have 5 brothers and 4 sisters. My mom stayed home to raise us, and that's a miracle in itself. I personally am not close with my dad, but I deeply respect him for putting his heart into his family. I am the only in my family to go away to college, but I did not attend college for the reasons you would think. I never wanted to be the first. I have accomplished much more through speaking Arabic than any of my brothers. I can read, write, and speak fluent Arabic. I am an avid reader of history and all things surrounding the eastern Mediterranean region. My brother's always gave me the role of translator and a lot of pressure came with that. I started to learn the ways of the almighty because I thought I had to. I started to read history, philosophy, religion, and culture of the world and the human race. I fell in love with it. I fell in love with knowledge and learning and humanity and we all are so blessed to be here. (More on questions like your greatest strengths and weaknesses and why Boeing)

Eventually I made it to college and I'm on my second degree. My first degree was in political science and my second degree will be in engineering.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
Well I grew up in New Jersey, but later in my early teens, we moved a bit to the south and then back north. I got very involved with sports, wrestling and football being my favorite and I was good at them. I also enjoyed creative writing, something I was good at but never felt strong enough to go forward with. I always thought that my writing wouldn’t mean much to the world, but after a few people read it I started to feel a little stronger about it. I still felt that only a few people would ever read it though and that stopped me from feeling too confident in my skills. My dad told me I would never do much with it and I believed him too. So I ended up taking his advice. Even though he was wrong and I have since realized I could have been more with what I was capable of, I learned from it.

For me what matters the most is my family and friends. I’ve always been about them before myself and I still am. I do my best to be the best family man and friend I can be to everyone I care about. I also enjoy football very much. I played it for a long time. I know a lot about football and enjoy watching it. In fact, I enjoy sports in general. Every sport but golf lol. I love to watch games, play games, do fantasy leagues. It is just something I feel strongly about and cannot see myself giving up. One of the things I like to do now is coach and mentor people who want to play football. You can say I still enjoy it and I like to pass on what I learned to the younger generation. I also really enjoy my job which takes up a lot of time. I work in a steel mill and it is something I do not want to leave. I like to spend time with my family and I love to play poker with them as well.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I am a USC by birth. My mother is a US citizen and my father is a naturalized citizen from India. I grew up in a close knit home with my three siblings (bigger bro and two sisters). All of us grew up pretty well grounded. Both my folks came here at a time when being here was harder than it is now. The second generation has grown up to take that for granted though. I come from a rather boring upbringing. There is nothing I can remember from when I was young that I loved. I do not remember going anywhere with my family when I was little and I never saw an amusement park until I was in my teens. The only thing I really remember from that time was the introduction of video games and my first visit to Sesame Place. It was so exciting to be able to "have" so much fun. I did not even learn to read until I was 11 (6th grade).

I was the geek that was not good at sports and excelled in academics. In high school, I was not the Jock or the geek (I hate labels). I was somewhat decent at sports. I ran track (400m dash) and once completed a 5K. I took a lunchtime job as a crossing guard and earned 125 bucks a month, and I also worked evenings after school as a grounds keeper. I loved the opportunity to work the grounds and taking care of the landscaping, it was pretty calming and therapeutic.

By my Senior year, the work on the grounds was no more and I would make extra money coaching kids at rec center (I am currently a certified referee in three sports, slow pitch softball, soccer and basketball). My time at high school was largely out and about exploring the town and talking to friends.

The next four years at college were much like high school just with way more alcohol consumption and the ability to explore a bit further away from home. I basically got my business degree by drinking and partying (wasn't that hard to do at a party school) and graduated with honors but no direction. So, about a year later I packed my bags (also brought along a lifetime supply of Mountain Dew and video games) and headed west to this huge technocratic community that people told me was the future.

I have always loved technology and at 18 I started buying computers, then networking equipment and then software. I created my first online business in college (1995) and quickly fell in love with technology. By the time I made it to California it was all consuming. So many opportunities.

The next 7 years passed rather quickly as I came to terms with the fact that the guy I wanted to be was no longer how I was living. There was a hunger and a passion to do and be more but it was hard to change your ways. My father passed away in my early 20's and my mother was not in good health. When we went for her surgery there was no discussion on keeping her alive or not. It was a very humbling experience and ultimately drove me to make a change in my life and what I wanted to be. For the first time in my life, I got a direction and started listening to people that were pushing me in that direction (not just telling me what I wanted to hear). It took a good year before I saw what my goal was. During that period, I wanted to be more than just a face in the crowd, a ho hum story. I knew that I wanted to excel, contribute, create and be a powerful force. I know have become exactly what I set out to be. The technology guru that is an expert in all kinds of platforms, is able to identify which solution will achieve the most effective and efficient result, to teach others what it is they need to know and to continue to explore new trends and evaluate them. I am 100% at the mercy of the technology industry. It is an ever evolving beast that continues to excite me and frighten me at the same time. I am not afraid of the work that it requires and the sleepless nights that are associated with it, I do not get frustrated by the most challenging and complex systems to ever be created or by the complex problems associated with their maintenance and support. I love my work and thrive when I can finally be right about something (to know that everything I have learned is well grounded).

So, after working in the corporate world for several years and establishing myself in the Silicon Valley I decided to take a big risk and move my family to a tiny little town (a half hour away) and start all over. We sold everything we had, packed the van and headed to Atlanta. It has been about 6 years and counting. The opportunity for my wife to continue to work has been great (she grew tired of the Silicon Valley) and the opportunity for me to have my creativity feed is what it takes. We are growing to love the suburbs and the concept of raising my children somewhere that is better for them. Many times I get the question, "Why don't you just stay there." and the answer is because Atlanta does not yet have the Tech scene that I am used to and my career has been centered around that scene. I have been afforded the opportunity to begin to develop it and slowly create my own impact in this big industry.

I have worked for some great companies here and moved around a lot. Time Warner, Accenture, Delta, Monster, 3M and currently HP have been some of the places that have given me the freedom to use my creativity. HP has afforded me the greatest potential for impact that I have ever had and I am very lucky to have the opportunity to thrive in this environment.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I turned 26 years old on May 29th of 2012. I grew up in San Jose, California. I lived there from ages 1 to 17. I was a healthy 9 pounds 2 ounces when I was born. My father was a Marine serving on his 4th combat tour during Vietnam. Because of that my mother would have to put me on a boat with a baby sitter in her stomach (whom was my future aunt) and leave from her house to San Francisco then transfer over to a boat that would take her out to the ferry to Marin. As soon as she got to Marin my aunt and grandmother would pick her up and carry her from the ferry into the island. When the tour was over my father came back from the war and my mother had me and I am who I am today because of my parents, their love, and their struggles. Things that are important to me are my family, friends, my work, doing things right the first time, and education. One of the best feelings in the world is doing something right the first time. Working hard is important and succeeding in what you are trying to accomplish. Giving back what you have learned to those who want to learn from you is extremely important because it will allow you to learn and understand the subject matter more when you are trying to teach it to another person. Education is everything; get it in the best way you know how. You can never learn too much. Education will lead you to your dreams, which is why it is so important.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am a 29-year-old U.S. Army combat veteran. I was born in Corpus Christi, Texas, and grew up in Kansas City, Missouri. My childhood was fairly uneventful, except for the fact that I was extremely introverted and anti-social. I didn’t make friends until I was a teenager, and even then, I wasn’t all that good at it. I was extremely shy, anxious, and afraid of a lot of things. I didn’t get into the Army until I was 24, after college, and it’s really where I grew into my own. I was able to leave my comfort zone and become the man I always knew I could be. I took control of my life and became the person I wanted to be.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I’m 29. I grew up in southern Arizona. My family and I moved around between three different cities before settling down in Tucson in 1999. The largest city I grew up in was Benson, which is a small community just south of Tucson in the deserts of southeastern Arizona. I was a person of fairly meager means and had very little exposure to opportunities. But I think, because of that, I remained a person who could see outside of the box in many ways. It’s hard to describe but I’ll try to do my best. Growing up, I had no real sports to speak of. Basketball, football, swimming, baseball—none of that. We just didn’t have the money to do it and my parents didn’t care about sports. Nothing was important to my parents as much as a good education. And so, growing up, I went to church every week and I went to school every day. My parents allowed me to take more of an interest in my education and I think that brought me a lot of opportunity. I ended up being very close to my school librarian (this was around the time when they had people in the schools who took care of books and other intellectual property) and was allowed to check out several books a week. I read a lot of science fiction, novels about heroic action and adventure, and just about anything else I could find that I thought might lead me into a place of success when I grew up. And all of these things matter a great deal to me. One of the things I value the most is the power to change. And all of the experiences I’ve had up to this point in my life have brought me to the point that I am at now.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I am 25 years old and I grew up in the Deep South. If you believe what you see on TV, then the Deep South is filled with racist white people, ignorant and uneducated, and obsessed with guns and country music. That’s just not true for the most part. People are people no matter where you go. We all have our struggles and our demons. We all have things that keep us up at night. I grew up a queer kid in the Deep South, and the one time I told my parents (at 16), they kicked me out of the house. I’ve been on my own ever since. I’ve dealt with my share of homophobia, biphobia, transphobia, and all of that bullshit. Being queer comes with a lot of invisible labels that people put on you. That’s a big reason why I started Untypical Blackness, so I could take away the labels and just be me. I didn’t want people to see me as a queer man or a Black man. I just wanted to be “Wes.” I guess I’m fortunate that the people around me have always been cool with who I am. As for what matters most to me, people would probably be surprised to learn that the answer is “being poor.” I’ve been poor for most of my life, not just financially, but emotionally and mentally. I was pretty sad and isolated for most of my 20s until Untypical Blackness came along and introduced me to all these wonderful people online. Having friends I can always count on has been one of the best things that’s ever happened to me.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am 24 years old, and I grew up in Brooklyn, NY. I think that my passion for helping others and my work ethic as well as my creativity stem from my mother. My mother is a strong, loving woman who was a single parent raising two children and a special needs daughter. She had her children late in life, and when I grew up, she wanted to be a psychologist. I can recall when she started college at age 46, and the struggles that she endured in her life. While getting her degree, she would often say that she had to keep the faith and never lose hope. My older sister was born with a developmental disorder called Mild Mental Retardation, which resulted in various developmental delays as well as behavioral problems. When my sister was born, my mother was overwhelmed with how unprepared she was for my sister to have these challenges and disabilities. She knew that the world had a lot of expectations, but she was unaware of how to properly care for her needs. I believe that she cared for her daughter because of her passion. As my mother went to school and worked a full-time job, she taught me the significance of the individual as well as the importance of knowing one’s self. My mother didn’t fully comprehend my personal goals as a teenager. However, I recall times when my mother would sit me down and go over different school, political, and religious ideas and ideologies that differed from her beliefs as well as mine. She often took the time to explain the history of her ideas and beliefs as well as providing me with the opportunity to formulate my own views and observations, and express them. I believe this is an invaluable parenting tactic that all parents should do. Everyone likes to feel as if they are being heard, and understood. My mother helped shape me into who I am today, and is someone that I deeply admire and respect.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: My name is Anna Kasa and I grew up in Sacramento, California. I am 23 years old. I was born in Azerbaijan but immigrated to California when I was 4. I grew up in a loving family. I was raised in a Christian home with parents who spent 3 out of 4 week nights having devotions after dinner. Also my parent’s and grandparents would take my siblings and I to church almost every week. My childhood is something to smile about. I always got along well with my siblings; my parents were very strict but it never got in the way of my love for them. I have always loved to read; I would often read until it was time for me to go to bed. In the summer I would climb trees, ride bikes, play soccer and play in the rain. In the winter I would sit by the fire. In high school I took on many responsibilities because I was a board member of many activities and groups. One day my volleyball coach got ill and I was in charge of coaching my team. I loved this very much. I was also in an American Sign Language Club, Link Crew, and I ran for student body office, which I am very thankful that I got. During high school I enjoyed learning about business, so much so that I have been on the honor roll since freshman year. I work and attend school. I just completed an internship where I worked with numbers, statistics, and etc. which I really liked. I hope my education and work experience can help me obtain a job here at C3 with your organization.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 26 years old. I grew up in a small town in Southern Illinois called DuQuoin. I was born in the time of AIDs, so my parent got flu shots while they were pregnant with me. I love being outdoors, I enjoy drawing, and spend way too much time with my dog Leo. I wouldn’t consider myself an independent person, I’ve always been more reserved and quiet. I’m not much for small talk, and always feel a bit self-conscious about putting myself out there.

Just about everyone I’ve come into contact with in my life (people in high school, family members, and co-workers alike) has had something negative to say about me. These comments have been made in attempts to hurt or belittle me in the heat of an argument. I’ve tried to take the time to understand where they’re coming from and taken their criticism to improve myself. Almost everyone is critical of me, but for different reasons. My father disliked the way I dressed. My coworkers said I was shy. Friends said I was too awkward or too quiet. For a time, I tried to change myself, but it doesn’t work. I found myself making good friends and being happy, but I felt out of place and began overthinking my interactions with people. I had friends before, but nothing to that extent. I became acquainted with my current friend group through Dungeons & Dragons at my local comic shop. The group was decently sized, and met at the shop every other week. The group slowly dissolved as people either didn’t find time for D&D or could not attend due to other commitments.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I am a 23 year old graduate student of economics. I was born and raised in the western suburbs of Chicago, IL. After high school, I graduated with an associates degree with honors. In college I focused on cognitive science, a fascinating subject which involves the study of how the brain stores and processes information. My work at the university research lab there yielded useful results that led me to submit a publication to an academic journal. My study focused on the neuroscience behind a cognitive bias called the 'coherence illusion' and identified a form of cognitive neurological weakness known as semantic dyspraxia. As a result, my paper, 'Quantifying the coherence illusion' was accepted for publication and has recently been released in the Theoretical Cognitive Neuroscience journal. All that work at the university led to a fantastic recommendation from my research advisor who is a prominent scientist in the field. I continue to work as a research assistant in her laboratory where I have worked since 2010. After moving on to graduate school, I felt that the upcoming tests would be a challenge because I have some specific weaknesses in their subject areas; in particular, I am currently working with a tutor to focus on my math and mechanical ability. My background in cognitive science has taught me to use careful, data-driven investigation to solve problems. My work at the university also taught me the value of working with others in a team environment and working in close cooperation with colleagues.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: Hello. My name is Chris. I’m the age of 32. In my mind I’m 21. In 23 years I have gone through many different things but I feel that I have learned from those things that made me who I am. I enjoy ice cream a whole hell of a lot. I have a daughter. She lives with her mom. I just enjoy spending time with her the most. And of course, family is the most important thing. At least to me it is. Money is not. I get paid a hell of a lot of money now and I’ve kept the same mentality. I’m here for you if you ever need me and I’m almost always available. Sure there will be times where I can’t be reached but that’s because I might be asleep or at work. Please do not hesitate to message me here and we can keep in contact.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 26 years old. Born and raised in Houston Texas.

I grew up with my wonderful parents and three sisters, a couple sisters are half sister, and a few sister in laws. I have 2 nephews, 1 nieces, and 2 goddaughters.

I’m a city gal. College educated, and community advocate. I'm known to stand my ground. My parents played a large role in my up bringing. My mom would always ask how can i better myself and help the community as well as myself, she taught us to be bold, have a voice and take risks. I have felt that my parents had an influence on me making a decision to change my race. I knew right from wrong, but I also knew that wanting to look differently or be part of another ethnicity is not wrong. It wasn’t an easy decision and the idea didn’t just appear one day. It took a long time before I got the courage to make such a drastic change.

I can remember always wanting to be a different race. I was 19 years old when I made the decision to completely change my race and do a skin color change and that's how far back I can remember. When I made the decision I wasn’t aware of what was considered to be taboo and frowned upon.

I decided that I needed to get a better education as a child so that I could find myself in a great career and have a better life and lifestyle. I thought if I better myself and focused more on helping others as well that I could be a strong influential figure in my community. I have lived by the three C's; culture, charity, and confidence. It wasn't until later in life that I realized how unusual this all was but at this point in life it is what makes me who I am. As a teen I did quite a bit of research. There was a fair amount of information but not as much as there is now.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text:
I am 26 years old, and grew up in rural Kansas. Before moving to Houston, my father worked as an associate pastor at a Baptist church for over 25 years. Growing up in a rural community did not lend itself to career opportunities as I was the first person in my family to finish high school. I also made the mistake of getting heavily involved in drugs and alcohol, as it was difficult to find things to do in rural America.

In high school, I really enjoyed sports. I was a multi-sport athlete, even playing baseball and wrestling at the state level. This changed when I moved to college to follow my dream of being a hairstylist. I couldn’t focus, and fell prey to my old demons.

When I came to Houston, I became heavily involved in the church my dad started a few years earlier. I volunteered in the youth group, and ended up becoming close friends with a lot of the kids. For a few years, I felt like everything was normal and that I was coping. All my old demons had been put to rest. I continued to volunteer at the church as a volunteer, and then a youth coordinator. It was a lot of fun and I continued to enjoy being around the kids. Eventually, the church moved to it’s own building, and I fell into a depression, which ended up being the beginning of the end. Everything fell apart from there.

I was diagnosed with severe depression and anxiety, and eventually entered rehab in June 2016. After several months of treatment and ups and downs, I finally started to find a new normal. My new normal consisted of going to the gym daily, volunteering in the community, and staying busy and active. My addiction may have been gone, but the mental trauma remained. It took a long time, but I finally feel like I have found my true self.

I love my parents more than anything, and they have been my rock throughout everything. I enjoy learning and continuing to grow. Learning new things gives me motivation and fulfillment.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I’m 26, I’m from Córdoba in Southern Spain. My childhood and teen years revolved around sport and as an adult I went to the gym 5 times a week and played football and worked in sales, I loved my job and my boss told me I was a natural for sales because of my ‘charm’. I have a lot of close friends, most are much older than me (at least 10 years) but my best friend is the same age as me and we always supported and encouraged each other. The most important thing in life is family for me, and if I feel happy, secure, and looked after then I can sacrifice anything else for my family. I used to call my mom every day until she decided to charge me for calling outside of Spain, and since then I only contact her when it’s absolutely necessary (every few months, when someone from the family gets ill or when I want her help doing something for me). In terms of leisure, I’m pretty simple, I read books, I listen to audiobooks, I like to be alone a lot but I also enjoy going out with friends. My favorite sport is football and I’m a Liverpool supporter. I love watching movies and TV, I’m a big fan of David Lynch, Tom Hanks, Kevin Smith and Tarantino. The last thing that made me rethink my life and its priorities was my first child, she’s the only reason I’m doing the adult things I need to do.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy? Please describe in detail.
text: I’m currently 25 years old and I grew up in Florida. Growing up in Florida to me was pretty uneventful. I enjoyed having the beach as my backyard, and going camping in the Everglades with my parents but I never really felt like I belonged there. I met my first boyfriend when I was 17 and that’s when my world really opened up. I met some friends, did some amazing things like snowboard and go whitewater rafting. A little after that I met another guy who really turned my world upside down. He made me feel like I had a voice and I wasn’t just some small town bumpkin. When I was 22 I moved back to Florida with my boyfriend to go to school and 9 months later we split. It was then that I realized that I wasn’t happy with who I was. I spent the next 2 years on a self improvement journey which included lots of reflection and spiritual work. During this time I realized that I hated living in Florida and that I had never really lived. It was then that I decided to make the big move to California. I came out here for the first time in December 2015 for 4 days. I got to meet 4 different people who live here which I can honestly say is the entire reason I moved out here. I don’t think I could have done it alone and I doubt that I would have wanted to. I’ve always liked the ocean and being a kid I was always making up stories about mermaids. Living in California and living by the ocean is something I’ve always dreamed of but never thought I would get to do. So in February of this year I left my old life behind to start a new one here.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I’m 28, and grew up in Austin, TX. I don’t really like the suburbs. My parents divorced when I was 11, and I went to a public school. I was a science nerd in high school. In college I studied physics and math. When I was in college I met a woman. I was her first boyfriend, and her first sexual partner. It was really hard for me to figure out what to do, but she encouraged me to keep going. We got married and she became pregnant. It was really hard for me to figure out what to do. We moved to Kansas so she could attend medical school, and then we moved to Austin to be closer to family. I went to work for the National Security Agency. I was there for 10 years. When we moved back to Austin we had a baby girl. I left the NSA to stay home and take care of the baby. My daughter is now 7 years old. I left the NSA to stay home and take care of the baby. My daughter is now 7 years old. I left the NSA to stay home and take care of the baby. I was there for 10 years.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: My name is Sarah Burke. I am 23 years old and I grew up in the United States. My father is originally from Michigan and has been a web designer and programmer. I spent 18 years on the sidelines of plenty of IT lessons, and I have also dabbled in my own coding over the years. I was home-schooled, but I took most of my classes at a college with a high school curriculum. I took a lot of math classes and also have the ability to speak Chinese. I enjoy playing sports like basketball, golf and bowling. I’m an avid reader, although it has been a long time since I’ve had much time to pick up a book. I don’t have much free time, though, so I mostly make the most of what little time I have.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text: I’m 37 years old and I grew up in a middle class home in rural Minnesota. I have one brother 2 years younger than I am. I was always the “nice” kid at school. I put others needs ahead of my own. I was bullied and became a bully in retaliation. I was a “good” student. I graduated college with a degree in finance, but pursued no further education. I married and my wife and I had a daughter. I recently divorced, but we are still friends and remain on good terms. Family and church are the two most important things in my life. I love spending time with my daughter and I adore the time I get to spend with my church family. I work for a small business in a small town. We have 2 employees (myself included). The other employee is my brother. I really enjoy working with him and I feel fortunate that I get to spend so much time with him. We usually split our workday into halves. One day he handles all the car work and I handle the office work. The next day I handle all the car work and he handles the office. In that way we get twice as much work accomplished as we would have working separately.

question: Tell me about yourself. How old are you? Where did you grow up? What events made you who you are? What matters to you and what do you enjoy?
text:
I’m 39 years old, but with the youth spirit still in me. Born and raised in Tacoma, WA, and never lived anywhere else (well, until I moved into the dorms). I was raised with the ideas that family is the most important thing there is, and education is the first step to “happiness”. Everything else is just extra luxuries to make life easier. I’m lucky to have lived with great parents, and they have taught me all they know to survive in this harsh world. The only thing they might not have taught me was how to deal with emotion and social interaction. *looks up at the interviewer, as if asks for his forgiveness* I was sent to some schools, but don’t remember much about them. We lived in Tacoma all my life, with a few minor stops in Tacoma and Lakewood. After graduating from Rogers High School, I earned my Associates of Arts at Pierce College. Right after that, I jumped into the workforce and worked jobs I hated, making minimum wage at first. Now I work at QFC and make good money doing what I like, and what’s important to me. And what’s important to me, is family. I look forward to spending as much time as I can with them.

Event that I found important in my life would be my Grandma’s death. She was like a second mom to me and always trusted my decisions, even if they were wrong. She always had some sort of lesson to teach me, and I know my Grandma by heart (and not because I used to be her caregiver *looks at interviewer*). She also inspired me to find my better half in life. Though we are a long ways off, I am still looking for the right girl. Just trying to figure out where to look. Maybe it’s that cafe around the corner.

What does “happiness” mean to me? It’s really subjective, and all the answers I could think of is nothing for what it can be. Just think about it, a woman is asked to say “Happy Birthday” to her husband, but instead she says “Hell No!”. That’s called happiness to her, and I know some men that would love that response. So, I guess my answer to what makes me happy is what makes others happy, and trying to spend my time with my family who enjoy it. And whatever makes my girlfriend happy too. *looks at the interviewer again*

At a short glance, you probably noticed I live a very boring life, and that’s totally true. Just the boring guy in the cubicle next to you, but with some creativity to design a decent website. If I didn’t live the boring life, I wouldn’t have enough energy to be a serial fitness artist, trying to lose weight or gain weight, and always learning about something new like the human body, or about social media, a whole new world where people spend hours on end just trying to communicate and “friend” each other.

Why do I want to do Salesforce? It can be a challenging job, and you have to be creative with it. Where as my current job, customer service, is challenging and you have to be creative. Salesforce also has the challenge of sales and marketing, which seems like the most interesting field to work in. Who knows? Maybe that skill could make me the President or something. Not saying you have to be a master at it to do so, but it sure wouldn’t hurt. I really hope there’s no essays on the test, though, my fingers would be really sore.
